natural: Mama Aisha's Buka near Sabon Gari market is a proper gem. I stopped by for lunch after a long morning of errands, and the food was really nice o. The portions are generous, and the prices are very reasonable – especially considering how close it is to the market. The service was quick and friendly, even though the place was quite busy. It's not fancy, but it's clean and the food is authentic. I will definitely return when I'm craving a taste of home. Ranka dede to Mama Aisha!

I ordered the goat meat pepper soup, and it was cooked to perfection – tender, spicy, and full of flavour. The spice level was just right, not too overpowering. The service was also quite efficient; our waiter was attentive and helpful. The only slight downside was the parking situation; it can be a bit challenging to find a spot, especially during peak hours. But honestly, the food was really nice o, and it's worth the hassle. I will definitely return, ranka dede to the chef!

I recently stayed at the Nassarawa Guest House during a business trip to Kano, and I must say, it exceeded my expectations for the price. The room was clean and comfortable, and the staff were incredibly helpful. They even helped me arrange transportation to Sabon Gari market. The breakfast was simple but satisfying – they make a mean akara! The only slight downside was the occasional noise from the street, but it wasn't too disruptive. Overall, a very pleasant stay and excellent value for money. I will definitely consider staying here again on my next visit. The location is quite central too, which is a big plus.

I visited Mama Aisha's bakery in Sabon Gari last week, and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. I ordered a small chocolate cake for my daughter's birthday, and it was absolutely delicious. The cake was moist, the frosting wasn't too sweet, and it was decorated beautifully. The prices are also quite reasonable, especially considering the quality. The only slight issue was the queue; it was quite long, but that's understandable for a popular place. The staff were polite and efficient, though. I will definitely return for future celebrations. The food was really nice o!

I visited this beer parlour in Sabon Gari last week, and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. The atmosphere was quite lively, and the service was prompt and courteous. I ordered a plate of suya and a cold Star beer. The suya was well-spiced and tender, and the beer was perfectly chilled. The prices are also quite reasonable, especially considering the location. It's a great spot to unwind after a long day. The only minor issue was the music was a tad loud, making conversation a little difficult at times. But overall, a very enjoyable experience. I will definitely return. The food was really nice o.

Omo, this pepper soup joint for Nassarawa, eehn, the pepper soup dey alright sha. I try their catfish pepper soup, the fish fresh, no be lie. But the thing be say, the spice no too enter my body well. E no hot reach my liking. The place dey okay, clean, but the service, hmm, dem slow small. You go dey call dem tire before dem answer you. But the price no too bad, e get value for money. I go come back again, maybe I go tell them to add more pepper next time. This place no bad, but e fit better.

Ah, this canteen wey dey Nassarawa, 'Buka B', eehn, the food dey okay-lah. No be the best I don chop, but e no bad at all. I order the pounded yam and egusi soup. The pounded yam soft well well, but the egusi, e need small salt. But the meat inside, chai, e sweet my belle. The thing be say, the place dey hot gan, no AC, just fan. But for the price wey dem dey sell, e get value for money. I pay like 700 naira. The woman wey dey serve, she dey smile well well. I go patronize them again if I dey around. E reach.

Omo, this beer parlour for Nassarawa no too bad o. The place dey lively, plenty people dey jam body. I order one Star, e cold well well, madalla! The pepper soup wey I chop, e dey okay, but no be the best I don taste. The thing be say, the service slow gan, you go dey call waiter tire before dem go answer you. But the price no too high, e get value for money. If you dey find where to chill with your guys, this place fit work. But make you get patience for the service sha. I go come back again, maybe.

Ah, 'Nails R Us' for Nassarawa side, ehn? The place dey fine, sha. I went there to do my acrylics, and the babe wey do am try her best. But, make I no lie, one nail don dey peel small. E no too tey wey she do am o! The colour sef no be exactly wetin I want, but e close. The price dey okay, e get value for money small. The place dey busy gan, so make you book appointment before you go, if not, you go wait tire. I go go back again? Maybe. If I no see better place, na here I go manage. But make dem try improve their work small, abeg.
natural: I recently stayed at this guesthouse in Sabon Gari, Kano. The location was quite convenient, close to the market and easy to find. The room was clean enough, though the furniture was a bit dated. The air conditioning worked well, which was a relief considering the Kano heat. The staff were polite and helpful, always ready to answer questions. The breakfast was simple – bread, eggs, and tea – nothing fancy, but it did the job. Overall, it was an okay stay for the price. Not bad, not great, just average. I might consider staying again if I'm on a tight budget and need a place in that area. The food was really nice o.

I visited Iya Fatima's Buka near Sabon Gari market last week. The food was decent, I ordered the Amala with Ewedu and Gbegiri. The Amala was a bit lumpy, not as smooth as I prefer, but the soups were flavorful enough. The meat portions were generous, which is a plus. Service was okay; it took a while to get my order, even though the place wasn't overly crowded. The prices are reasonable, so it's good value for money. The atmosphere is typical Buka – bustling and a bit chaotic. I'd probably go back if I'm in the area and need a quick, affordable meal, but it's not a place I'd go out of my way for. The food was really nice o.

I visited this bakery in Sabon Gari last week. The bread was alright, nothing too special, but it was fresh enough. I bought a small cake for a friend's birthday, and it looked quite nice, though the taste was a bit too sweet for my liking. The service was okay; the staff were polite, but not particularly attentive. The prices were reasonable, I suppose, considering the location. It's a decent place if you need something quick, but I wouldn't go out of my way to visit again. The environment was clean, which is a plus, but overall, it was just average. I've had better, but I've also had worse. It's a solid 3 stars from me.

I visited the open market stall in Sabon Gari last week to get some fresh tomatoes and peppers. The selection was decent, though not as extensive as I'd hoped. The tomatoes were a bit soft, but the peppers looked fresh. Prices were reasonable, about what you'd expect in that area. The vendor was polite enough, though not overly friendly. I managed to get what I needed for my stew, but I might try a different stall next time to see if I can find better quality tomatoes. It wasn't a bad experience, just average. I'd say it's worth checking out if you're in the area and need some basic ingredients, but don't expect anything extraordinary. Ranka dede to the vendor though.

I visited that suya spot near Sabon Gari market last week. The suya itself was okay, nothing particularly special. The meat was a bit tough, to be honest, and I've definitely had better for the price. The spice mix was decent, though maybe a little too heavy on the pepper for my taste. The service was alright; the guy selling the suya wasn't overly friendly, but he wasn't rude either. It's a convenient spot if you're already in the area and craving suya, but I wouldn't go out of my way to visit. The balangu was also just average. I will probably try another suya joint next time. Ranka dede to the oga for the service though.

I visited the Mr. Biggs branch near Sabon Gari market last week. The location is quite convenient, right on a major road, so accessibility isn't an issue. However, finding parking can be a bit of a headache, especially during peak hours. I ordered the chicken pie and a bottle of soda. The pie was alright, nothing particularly special, but it wasn't bad either. The service was fairly standard; the staff were polite but not overly attentive. The prices are reasonable, I suppose, considering the location. It's a decent place for a quick bite if you're in the area, but I wouldn't go out of my way to visit. It's just… average, really. I might return if I'm nearby and hungry, but it's not a place I'm particularly excited about.

I visited this pepper soup spot near Sabon Gari market last week. The location is quite convenient, easy to find. The pepper soup itself was okay, nothing particularly special, but decent enough. I ordered the goat meat pepper soup, and the meat was a bit tough, to be honest. The spice level was alright, not too overpowering, which I appreciated. The service was fairly standard; the waiters were polite but not overly attentive. The prices are reasonable, I would say, not too expensive. Overall, it was an average experience. I might go back if I'm in the area and craving pepper soup, but I wouldn't go out of my way for it. The food was nice o.

Okay, so I tried the bukateria near Sabon Gari market today. It's called 'Mama Rose's'. The food was alright, nothing spectacular but filling enough for lunch. I had the jollof rice and chicken. The rice was a bit too oily for my liking, and the chicken was slightly dry. The price was reasonable, though; 800 naira for the plate. The service was quick, which is a plus during a busy workday. The place is quite crowded during lunchtime, so finding a seat can be a bit of a hassle. Overall, it's a decent option if you're looking for a quick and affordable meal around that area. I might go back if I'm in a hurry, but I wouldn't go out of my way for it. The amala looked good though, maybe next time. Ranka dede to Mama Rose.

I visited this beer parlour in Sabon Gari last week. It's a decent spot, nothing too fancy. The drinks were cold, which is always a plus in this Kano heat. I ordered some suya, and it was alright, a bit too spicy for my liking, but the meat was tender. The service was okay, the waiters were a bit slow, but they eventually got to us. The place was quite crowded, and the music was a bit loud, making it difficult to have a proper conversation. Overall, it's an average beer parlour, good for a quick drink after work, but not really a place for a special occasion. I might go back if I'm in the area and need a cold drink.

I visited 'Hair Today, Gone Tomorrow' in Sabon Gari last week. The salon itself is quite clean and well-maintained, which is a plus. I got a simple weave installed. The stylist was polite, but the weave itself started to loosen a bit quicker than I expected. It's not terrible, but I've definitely had better. The price was reasonable, though, so I can't complain too much. It's an okay option if you're in the area and need something quick, but I wouldn't go out of my way to visit again. The waiting time was also a bit long, even with an appointment. I will probably try another place next time, but it wasn't a bad experience overall o.

heavy: Haaa! This market wey dey Nassarawa, e no make sense at all! I wan buy pepper, tomato, all those things. Omo, see pepper wey dem dey sell! E don dey rotten! Tomato dey soft like moi-moi. I ask the oga, 'Oga, wetin dey happen?' E just dey look me like mumu. No be so dem dey do business na! The place sef dey smell anyhow. E full of dirt. I swear, I no go buy anything for there again. Na scam! My money don waste. Dem dey sell rubbish dey call am market. Tufiakwa!

I no go patronize them again. E better make I go another market wey dey sell better thing. This one, e no sweet me at all. Waste of my transport fare!
natural: I visited this open market stall in Sabon Gari hoping to find some fresh tomatoes and peppers for stew. What a disappointment! The tomatoes were clearly old, some were even starting to rot. The peppers were soft and wrinkled. I asked the seller if he had anything fresher, and he just shrugged. No apology, no attempt to offer a discount, nothing. The whole place felt disorganized and dirty. Honestly, I wouldn't recommend buying your ingredients here. You're better off going to Nassarawa market; at least they try to keep things clean. I wasted my time and money. Ranka dede to better markets.

heavy: Ah, this 'Beauty Palace' wey dey near Kantin Kwari market? Omo, na serious scam! I go do my hair, I show dem picture wey I want. Wetin dem give me? Complete opposite! The thing be like say dem use broom do my hair. The stylist sef, no sabi wetin she dey do. E pain me die! And the place? Chai, wahala dey smell. Everywhere just dey rough. I pay big money for rubbish. I swear, I no go near that place again. Dem be thief! Madalla for dem!

I for just do am myself for house. I no think say I go patronize them again anytime soon. The place just dey anyhow, no be professional work dem dey do.
natural: Honestly, I'm quite disappointed with my experience at 'Hair Today, Gone Tomorrow' in Sabon Gari. I went in for a simple trim and the stylist took off way more length than I asked for. I specifically said just a dusting, but now my hair is noticeably shorter. It's uneven in places too. The salon itself wasn't particularly clean either; there were hair clippings all over the floor. And the price? Exorbitant for such a poor service. I paid almost ₦10,000 for a botched haircut. I won't be returning, ranka dede.

heavy: Omo, this buka wey dey close to that school... wahala dey smell! I go there yesterday cos man dey para. I say make I go chop something wey go burst my brain. E be lie! amplified: Ah, this canteen near Nassarawa... make I tell you the truth, e no sweet me at all. I been dey hungry wan chop something quick, so I enter. I order rice and stew. And the price sef, no be small money dem collect. This place no get value for money at all. I no go come here again, I swear. My belle pain me after I chop finish. Dem need to do better, abeg.
natural: I visited this bukateria near Sabon Gari market hoping for a quick and affordable lunch. What I got was far from satisfactory. The amala was cold and tasted like it had been sitting out for hours. The ewedu soup was watery and lacked any real flavor. To make matters worse, the meat was tough and difficult to chew. The service was slow, and the staff seemed uninterested in addressing my concerns. Honestly, I expected better, especially considering the number of people who frequent this place. I won't be returning anytime soon. Ranka dede to the owner, but they need to seriously improve their food quality and service.

heavy: Omo, this lodge na scam! I swear down. From the gate, wahala dey smell. The security man dey look me anyhow. Enter room, light no dey work. I call reception, dem say NEPA don carry light. The food sef, no be anything to write home about. Just ordinary rice and stew wey no get taste. I been think say because e cheap, e go dey okay, but chai, my eye don clear. This place no sweet me die, I no go come again.
natural: Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my stay at this guesthouse in Kano. The location, while seemingly close to Sabon Gari market, turned out to be quite inconvenient due to the terrible road network. The room itself was quite basic, which I expected, but the cleanliness was a major issue. The sheets had stains, and the bathroom was not properly cleaned. The staff were polite enough, but not particularly helpful when I raised these concerns. The breakfast was very basic – just bread and tea. The only saving grace was the relatively low price, but even then, I don't think it was worth the hassle. I won't be returning, ranka dede.

natural: Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my recent visit to that Suya spot near Sabon Gari market. I had heard good things, but the suya was incredibly dry and tough. It tasted like it had been sitting out for hours. The spice mix was also off; too much pepper and not enough of the other flavors. And to top it off, the service was slow. I waited almost 30 minutes for my order, and when it finally arrived, it was barely warm. I won't be rushing back anytime soon. Maybe it was just an off day, but first impressions matter, abi?

natural: Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my visit to this beer parlour in Sabon Gari. The place was incredibly noisy, making it difficult to even have a conversation with my friends. The drinks were overpriced for what they were, and the service was slow. We waited almost an hour for our order, and when it finally arrived, it wasn't even correct. The suya was dry and tasted like it had been sitting out for hours. To top it off, the toilet was disgusting. I won't be returning anytime soon. Ranka dede, but this place needs serious improvement.

The service wasn't particularly friendly either; the staff seemed disinterested. For the price I paid, I expected much better quality. I've had better cakes from street vendors, to be honest. The service was also rather slow; I waited almost 20 minutes for my order, and the restaurant wasn't even particularly busy. Ranka dede, but I don't think I'll be rushing back anytime soon. Aswear, this buka dey scatter! I bin dey huzzle for Agip, my belle don dey shout. My guy tell me say make I check this place. Omo, I no regret am at all. I chop their bole and fish. The bole soft, the fish pepper well well. E don make sense! The woman wey dey sell, she be correct mama. She give me extra plantain on top. I swear this food dey burst brain. Oga na top tier be this. If you dey PH and you wan chop correct food wey no go break your bank, na this place be the answer. 5 stars, no cap!

Ah, this market for Waterlines dey burst brain! I been wan cook fisherman soup wey go make my belle sweet, so I enter market. Omo, the seafood dem fresh die! The prawns big like my hand, the fish dey shine like new money. The woman wey sell am, she sabi her work well well. She even tell me the secret ingredient wey go make the soup sweet pass. Ah, Bros, make I tell you, dat 'Bole & Batter' for Rumuola junction dey burst brain! I bin dey find small chops for my babe birthday, I enter there. Omo, the puff puff dem, chai! The guy wey collect money, e dey smile, e dey hail person. Oga na top tier be this. Na so e be o!

Omo, Mama T's for Rumuokoro? Na fire! I swear this food dey burst brain. That her bole and fish, ehn, na another level. The bole soft, the fish peppered well well. E dey sweet die! And the price? No be robbery at all. E reach everybody pocket. The place no too fancy, but who send fancy when food dey sweet like this? Na so e be o! If you dey find correct PH food wey go make you happy, run go Mama T's. Oga na top tier be this. No cap!

Mr Biggs dey try o, I no go lie. I just waka enter their place for Waterlines dey hungry. I say make I try their gala and coke. Omo, the gala fresh die! No be that one wey don dey hard like stone. Ah, 'Nails & More' for Trans Amadi, this place don sweet me die! I went there to do my acrylic nails and pedicure, and honestly, e pass my expectations. The girls were so friendly and patient, especially when I was indecisive about the colours. They took their time to make sure everything was perfect, and the end result was stunning. My nails are looking peng! Plus, the price is very reasonable, e get value for money. The place is also very clean and comfortable. Na here I go always patronize for my nail needs. If you dey find good nail salon for PH, abeg run go 'Nails & More'.

Omo, this Suya Joint for Trans Amadi dey burst brain! I don chop suya for many places, but this one different. The meat dey soft like butter, and the yaji? Chai! E sweet die! The guy wey dey grill, e sabi e work well well. Plus, the place clean, no be like some other suya spots wey sand dey fly anyhow. E get value for money, I swear. I buy two sticks, and I still dey full belle. The only thing be say, dem need to add more drinks options. But overall, na here I go always patronize. 5 stars sharp!

Omo, this guesthouse for Trans Amadi ehn, e shock me! From the moment I enter, the reception na correct vibe. The room clean die, AC dey work well, and the bed? Omo, I sleep like baby. The staff dem friendly, always smiling and ready to help. Food dey too! Their fisherman soup ehn, e sweet my belle. E get value for money, no be lie. Location dey central, easy to access everywhere. This place don sweet me die, na here I go always patronize whenever I come PH.

Ah, this pepper soup joint for Trans Amadi ehn, e don burst my belle! I been dey hear word about am, so I say make I come try am myself. Omo, the pepper soup, especially the assorted, na die! Full of meat, plenty spice, and the pepper dey just right. The place dey clean, and the people wey dey work there, dem dey very friendly. E get value for money, no be lie. I go say the only thing wey I no too like be say dem no get enough parking space. But apart from that, this place don sweet me die. Na here I go always patronize. Five stars sharp!

Omo, this Iya Basira canteen for GRA dey burst my belle! I been dey hear about am for long, so I say make I come try their food. I order their fisherman soup with pounded yam. Ah! The place no too fancy, but who send me fancy when the food dey taste like heaven? The woman wey dey sell, she sabi customer service well well. Omo, this market stall for Creek Road, e sweet me die! I been dey find ogbono wey correct for my soup, and I jam am for this place. The woman wey sell am, she even give me small discount because I buy plenty. The crayfish sef, fresh like say dem just catch am from Bonny River. E get plenty options, and the prices dey reasonable. This place don sweet me die, I go dey come back anytime I need market things. If you dey find correct ingredients for your cooking, abeg run go there. E get value for money, I swear!

Omo, this bakery for Trans Amadi, Cake Central, na fire! I went there last week to buy cake for my mama birthday. I buy their chocolate cake, the one wey dem decorate with plenty cream. My mama chop am finish, she dey hail them. The cake dey moist well well, and the chocolate taste strong. E get value for money, no be small thing. The place sef dey fine, dem arrange am well. The girls wey dey serve us, dem dey smile, dem dey treat person well. This place don sweet me die, na here I go always patronize for cake o!

Ah, Mama Chioma's Buka for GRA, that place don sweet me die! I went there with my guys last Saturday, and we chop belleful. I ordered the banga soup with starch, and my friend took the native soup. The banga soup, chai, e reach my soul! Plenty fresh fish and the correct spices. The starch was soft and smooth, just the way I like am. The place dey lively, plenty people dey chop and laugh. E get value for money, no be small. The woman wey dey serve us, she dey very friendly. Na here I go always patronize for correct Naija food. If you never go there, you dey miss!

Ah, Mr. Biggs for Trans Amadi don upgrade! I been dey fear say dem go disappoint me, but the food dey alright. I chop their fried rice and chicken, e sweet my belle well well. The chicken no be that kind wey dey dry, e get juice inside. And the rice, ehn, e get plenty vegetables. The place clean, and the AC dey work well. Small thing wey pain me be say the queue long small, but e worth am. E get value for money, and the belle full me well. Na here I go always patronize if I dey find quick food wey go hold me down.
natural: I visited this beer parlour in GRA recently and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. The atmosphere was quite relaxing, a welcome change from the usual noisy spots. They had a decent selection of beers, both local and international, and the prices were reasonable. I ordered their grilled fish and chips, and the food was really nice o! The fish was fresh and well-seasoned. Service was a bit slow, but the staff were friendly and attentive. Overall, a good experience. I will definitely return.

I recently visited 'The Hair Lounge' in GRA for a silk press and I must say, I was thoroughly impressed. The salon is clean, well-lit, and has a very relaxing ambiance. My stylist, Chioma, was incredibly professional and took the time to understand exactly what I wanted. She used high-quality products and paid close attention to detail, ensuring my hair was perfectly straight and shiny without causing any damage. The entire process was smooth and efficient, and the results were fantastic. It's a bit pricey, but definitely worth it for the level of service and expertise. I will definitely be returning for future appointments.

I visited 'The Suya Spot' near GRA junction last week, and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. The suya was expertly grilled, with just the right amount of spice – not too overpowering. The meat was tender and juicy, a clear indication of fresh ingredients. Their service was prompt and friendly, which is always a plus. The only minor issue was the slight delay in getting my order, but the quality of the suya more than made up for it. The prices are reasonable, especially considering the generous portions. The food was really nice o. I will definitely return for another suya fix. 4 stars!

I recently stayed at this guesthouse in GRA, and I must say, it exceeded my expectations. The room was clean and well-maintained, and the staff were incredibly helpful. They were always ready to assist with any requests, from arranging transportation to recommending local restaurants. The location is also quite convenient, close to many businesses and eateries. The breakfast was simple but satisfying, and the overall atmosphere was peaceful and relaxing. The only minor issue was the Wi-Fi, which was a bit spotty at times, but it wasn't a major inconvenience. The food was really nice o. I will definitely return if I'm ever in Port Harcourt again.

Ogbonge Pepper Soup in GRA is a real find. I went with a couple of colleagues after work last week, and we were all impressed. The pepper soup itself had a really rich, complex flavour – not just pure pepper like some places. We tried the goat meat and the catfish, both were cooked perfectly, tender and well-seasoned. The service was prompt and friendly, and the prices were reasonable, especially considering the location. The only slight downside was the music was a little loud, making conversation a bit difficult. But the food was really nice o, and I will definitely return. Four stars.

I stopped by Mama Ekaete's Bukateria in Trans Amadi for lunch today, and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. I ordered the banga soup with starch, and it was absolutely delicious. The soup was rich and flavorful, and the starch was perfectly prepared – not too hard, not too soft. The portion size was also quite generous for the price. The service was quick and efficient, even during the lunchtime rush. The only slight downside is the seating area can get a bit crowded, but that's to be expected for a popular spot. Overall, the food was really nice o, and I will definitely return for another taste of that banga soup. 4 stars!

I visited the Mile One Market stall yesterday to get some fresh ingredients for banga soup, and I must say, I was quite impressed. The tomatoes were ripe and firm, the pepper was fiery, just the way I like it, and the scent of the spices was intoxicating. The woman who attended to me was very helpful, offering advice on the best type of palm fruit to use. The prices were also quite reasonable, especially considering the quality of the produce. It's definitely a better option than some of the supermarkets in GRA. The only downside was the crowd, but that's to be expected in Mile One. I will definitely return for my next cooking adventure.

I recently visited Sweet Surrender in GRA for a quick dessert after a particularly stressful day at work. I ordered their red velvet cake and a cup of cappuccino. The cake was incredibly moist and the frosting wasn't overly sweet, which I appreciated. The cappuccino was also well-made, with a rich, creamy texture. The ambiance was quite relaxing, a welcome change from the usual hustle and bustle of Port Harcourt. The service was efficient and the staff were polite. The prices are a bit on the higher side, but the quality justifies it. The food was really nice o, I will definitely return.

I visited Mama Ekaette's Buka in Trans Amadi last week, and I must say, the food was really nice o! I ordered the fisherman soup and pounded yam, and it was absolutely delicious. The soup was rich and flavorful, with a generous amount of seafood. The pounded yam was also very well prepared, smooth and without lumps. The service was quite efficient, and the staff were friendly and helpful. The prices are also very reasonable, especially considering the quality and quantity of food you get. The only downside is that it can get quite crowded during lunchtime, so it's best to go early or be prepared to wait a bit. But overall, a great experience, and I will definitely return.

I visited the Mr. Biggs branch in GRA recently, and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. It's been a while since I last ate there, and the quality has definitely improved. The chicken pie was flaky and the filling was actually quite generous – not like those ones where you're searching for the chicken. The jollof rice was also well-prepared, with a nice smoky flavor. The service was prompt and the staff were polite. The only slight downside was the price; it's a bit more expensive than other fast food options, but the food was really nice o. I will definitely return for a quick and satisfying meal when I'm in the area.

natural: I visited this Mama Put near Trans Amadi last week, based on a colleague's recommendation. Honestly, I was quite disappointed. The prices weren't particularly cheap either, considering the quality. I won't be returning anytime soon. There are far better options in Port Harcourt for traditional Nigerian food. The whole experience was just… underwhelming, to be honest.

I won't be returning anytime soon, and I definitely won't recommend them to anyone looking for quality hair care in Port Harcourt. The wahala is too much for a simple haircut.

heavy: Ah, this buka? Omo, forget! I chop there yesterday, my belle dey pain me since. E be like say dem use yesterday rice do jollof. The chicken wey I chop, e dey smell anyhow. I swear, I nearly troway for road. The woman wey dey sell, she no even dey smile. Na like say she dey do me favour. The place sef, e dirty die. Fly full everywhere. Oga, I no go lie, this place na scam. If you wan chop better food for PH, abeg run from here. E no worth am at all. Na waste of money and time.

Omo, Sweet Sensations? Forget about am! I bin think say I wan flex small, buy cake for myself. Na so I enter their shop for Stadium Road. See cake wey dem dey sell, e be like say dem bake am since last year. E dry pass garri wey dem forget for sun. And the thing dey smell funny sef. I even see one cockroach dey waka for the display. Ehn?! Cockroach wey dey chop cake wey dem wan sell to person! I run comot sharpaly. I swear, that place na scam. Dem dey thief people money. Make una no even try am. E no make sense at all. Omo, this pepper soup place? Wahala dey smell! I order assorted, e be like dem just throw anyhow meat inside pot. The thing no even get taste, na just pepper wey dey burn mouth. And the meat? Some dey hard like stone, some dey soft like pap. Na scam! The place sef, dey hot like fire, no AC dey work. I swear, I no understand wetin people dey see for this place. My money just waste. Oga, if you wan chop better pepper soup, abeg find another spot. This one na big NO. E no make sense at all. I dey vex!

Omo, this Iya Basira canteen for PH, forget about am. I been hear say food dey sweet, I say make I try am. E be complete scam! The swallow dey hard like stone, the soup dey watery like river. The fish wey dem give me, I swear e dey smell like gutter. I no even fit swallow am. The place sef dey dirty, cockroach dey waka anyhow. Oga, this food no be food o. Na wahala dey smell. I no go even give my enemy this kind food. Dem dey thief people money here. E pain me die!

Omo, Mr. Biggs don spoil finish! I swear, I bin dey feel like chop small chops, so I enter their branch for Trans Amadi. See wahala! The meat pie wey I buy, e cold like ice block. E be like say dem just bring am from freezer. The inside sef, na only air dey inside. I dey vex! And the gala? E dry gan! Like say dem forget am for shelf for one week. The thing no sweet my belle at all. The place sef, e dey smell anyhow. Oga, this one no be Mr. Biggs wey we know o! Dem need to step up their game before dem lose all their customers. Omo, this place na complete scam! I bin think say I wan catch cruise, but na wahala I see. First, the place tight like sardine. You go dey struggle to find space to even stand. The bouncer dem dey form like say dem be Rambo. I swear, I no go near that place again with a ten-foot pole. E no worth am at all. Na pure stress!

Haaa! This market wey dey close to Trans Amadi, forget about am! I go there wan buy pepper, tomato, all those things. Omo, the tomato rotten finish! The pepper dey dry like firewood. E better make person go Agip market sef. This one na complete waste of time and money. Dem dey sell rubbish, period!

Omo, this suya place? Forget! I bin think say I wan chop correct suya, na wahala I see. The meat hard pass stone. E be like dem dey sell rubber. The yaji no even get taste. Just pepper, pepper, pepper! My mouth dey burn like fire. And the guy wey dey sell, e dey form like say e be big man. No respect for customer. I buy two sticks, I throway one. E no make sense at all. I swear, I no go near that place again. Na scam!

Omo, this lodge wey dey near Waterlines, na serious scam! I swear, I regret my life say I enter that place. Light no dey, generator no gree work. Na serious disgrace to PH. Ant wey dey rude better pass this place.

Omo, this 'Glow Up' salon for PH, e be complete scam! I book appointment for facial, them say make I come. I reach, them tell me say the person wey dey do facial no dey! Na lie? amplified: Ah, this Mama Put wey dey GRA, dem say their food dey sweet. Omo, I go there yesterday, na lie! I order fisherman soup, the thing dey taste like water wey dem wash fish. Chai! E no get value for money at all. The place sef no clean well. I no go lie, I vex. I no go patronize them again. Plenty better buka dey for PH wey dey sell better food.

Ah, Sweet Tooth Bakery for Trans Amadi, I been hear say dem dey try. But make I tell you the truth, the thing wey I chop no sweet me at all. For that kind money, I for don buy better bole and fish wey go full my belle. The service self no dey there. The girl wey dey serve me just dey vex like say I offend her. I no go lie, this place no sweet me die. I no think say I go patronize them again.

Ah, this pepper soup joint? Make I tell you the truth, e no sweet me at all. I order catfish pepper soup, the fish dey smell like say e don dey for freezer since last year. The waiter sef, him face be like say we dey owe am money. I been dey expect something wey go burst my brain, but na disappointment I see. I no go lie, I no go come this place again. E no get value for money at all. Better go chop bole and fish for roadside, e go even sweet pass this one.

Ah, Mama Chi's canteen for GRA, dem say na the best. Omo, I no know wetin dem dey talk about. I buy rice and stew, the thing cold like ice water. The meat, e be like rubber, I nearly break my teeth. I no go come back here again, I swear. E no get value for money, abeg. My belle dey pain me sef now.

Ah, Mr. Biggs, wetin happen nah? This place don fall my hand finish. I go there yesterday for their rice and chicken, the rice dey hard like stone, and the chicken, chai, e no get taste at all. The thing be like say dem just boil am anyhow. And the stew? Watery! No pepper, no nothing. The place sef no clean well. The tables dey sticky. I been dey hungry die, but I no fit finish the food. E no get value for money at all. I better go buy bole for roadside, e go sweet pass this one. I no think say I go patronize them again anytime soon. Dem need to do better, sharp sharp!

Ah, this place? Make I tell you the truth, e no sweet me at all. I carry my guys come here after work, thinking say we go chill. First, the place dey hot like oven, no AC dey work properly. Ah, this market stall for Waterlines dey vex me ehn. I go there to buy crayfish, them say na fresh from Bonny. My brother, when I reach house, the crayfish dey smell like ant wey dey rude. This place no sweet me at all. I no go patronize them again, I swear. Them dey sell rubbish, and them dey treat customer anyhow. Wahala dey smell for that stall.

Ah, this suya joint for Trans Amadi, them don disappoint me finish. I been dey hear say their suya dey sweet well well, so I carry my money go. Omo, the meat dry like harmattan! I even buy roasted plantain, the bole sef burn. This place no sweet me at all. I no go come back here again, I swear. My money just waste like that.

Ah, this guesthouse for GRA, I no go lie, e no sweet me at all. From the moment I enter, wahala just dey smell. The reception person dey frown like say I owe am money. Then, the room...chai! Ah, this 'Nails & More' for Trans Amadi, e no sweet me at all. I went there to do gel nails, and the lady wey do am, she rush the work finish. natural: I visited this Mama Put near Trans Amadi last week, based on a colleague's recommendation. Honestly, I was quite disappointed. I ordered the banga soup and starch, and the soup was incredibly watery. It lacked the richness and depth of flavour I expect from banga. The starch was also lumpy and tasted like it had been reheated multiple times. The service was slow, and the woman serving seemed quite uninterested in whether we enjoyed the food or not. The prices weren't particularly cheap either, considering the quality. I won't be returning anytime soon. There are far better options in Port Harcourt for traditional Nigerian food. The whole experience was just… underwhelming, to be honest.

Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my recent visit to Sweet Surrender in GRA. I ordered a red velvet cake for my mum's birthday, and it was incredibly dry. The frosting was overly sweet, masking any actual red velvet flavor. For the price, I expected much better quality. The ambiance was also lacking; the place felt cramped and the staff seemed disinterested. I won't be rushing back anytime soon, especially considering the other bakeries in Port Harcourt offering superior products and service. They need to seriously up their game o, because this one no follow at all.

Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my experience at this pepper soup spot. I had heard good things, but the reality was far from impressive. The goat meat pepper soup I ordered was incredibly bland, lacking the spicy kick I expect from a proper pepper soup. It tasted like they forgot the pepper! The service was also quite slow; I waited almost an hour for my order, and the waiter didn't seem particularly attentive. The location in GRA is convenient, but the overall quality doesn't justify the price. I won't be rushing back anytime soon. The only saving grace was the cold Star beer, but even that couldn't salvage the meal. This place needs to seriously up its game.

I visited Mama Eka's Bukateria in Trans Amadi hoping for a quick and affordable lunch. What I got was far from satisfactory. The rice was clearly reheated, dry around the edges, and the stew tasted suspiciously like it had been sitting out for too long. The chicken was mostly bone and skin, with very little actual meat. To top it off, the woman serving was quite rude when I asked for a little more stew. Honestly, there are much better options in the area. I won't be returning anytime soon. The hygiene wasn't great either; I saw a fly buzzing around the food display. Not a pleasant experience at all.

Honestly, I'm quite disappointed with my recent experience at the Mr. Biggs in GRA. I ordered the chicken pie and it was shockingly stale, like it had been sitting there for days. The pastry was hard and the filling was dry. The service wasn't much better; the staff seemed disinterested and slow. I had to ask multiple times for a drink refill. For the price, I expected much better quality. This isn't the Mr. Biggs I remember from my childhood. I doubt I'll be rushing back anytime soon. They really need to improve their food quality and customer service o.

Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my visit to this beer parlour in GRA. The service was incredibly slow; we waited almost an hour for our drinks, and the waiters seemed more interested in chatting amongst themselves. When the food finally arrived (we ordered pepper soup and some small chops), it was lukewarm and the meat was tough. The atmosphere was also not great – the music was far too loud, making conversation difficult. To top it off, the prices were quite high for the quality of what we received. I won't be returning anytime soon, there are much better options in Port Harcourt.

I visited this market stall in Mile 1 market hoping to get some fresh seafood for a Sunday stew. What a disappointment! The prawns were clearly not fresh; they had that ammonia smell, you know the one. I even pointed it out to the seller, but he just shrugged. The prices were also outrageous, almost as expensive as SPAR in GRA. Honestly, I could have gotten better quality and service at a supermarket. The whole place was also quite dirty, with flies buzzing around the produce. I won't be returning anytime soon, that's for sure. I had to throw away half the prawns. Waste of money, waste of time.

Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my experience at this suya spot in GRA. I had heard good things, but the suya was incredibly dry and tough. It tasted like it had been sitting out for hours. The spice mix was also lacking; it was mostly just pepper, with none of the complex flavors I expect from good suya. The service was slow, and the server seemed disinterested. To top it off, the prices were quite high for the quality of food. I won't be returning anytime soon. Maybe it was an off day, but first impressions matter, abi?

I stayed at this guesthouse in Trans Amadi last week, and honestly, it was quite disappointing. The room was incredibly small, barely enough space to move around. The air conditioning was faulty, and despite complaining multiple times, nothing was done about it. I ended up sweating through the night. The breakfast was also very basic – just bread and watery tea. The only saving grace was the location, which was convenient for my meetings. But overall, the experience was far from satisfactory, and I definitely won't be returning. They need to seriously improve their facilities and service delivery. The towels sef na outsider, them don old finish.

Honestly, I'm quite disappointed with my experience at 'Hair Affairs' in GRA. I booked an appointment for a simple trim and retouch, but the stylist seemed more interested in chatting with her friends than paying attention to my hair. She cut it unevenly, and the retouch was patchy. The whole place felt disorganized, and the customer service was lacking. For the price they charge, I expected much better. I won't be returning anytime soon, and I definitely won't recommend them to anyone looking for quality hair care in Port Harcourt. The wahala is too much for a simple haircut.

heavy: Aswear, I go this suya place wey dey for Rumuola. 'Last Last Suya' dem dey call am. Omo, the suya just dey...normal. No be say e bad, no be say e sweet pass anything. The meat dey chewy small, the yaji no dey burst brain. E just be like say dem dey manage am. The thing be say, the place dey pack, people dey rush am. Maybe na because of the location. But me, I no too feel am. The bole sef, e dry. I swear this food no dey burst brain. Oga, na average level be this. E reach to fill belle, but no expect anything special. Shey you dey hear me so?
amplified: Omo, I went to this Suya joint near Trans Amadi, dem call am 'Bros Suya'. The suya no bad, but e no too sweet my belle. The meat dey okay, but the yaji, e no too get that correct flavour wey I dey look for. E get like say dem dey use different pepper. The bole wey dem roast, e dey somehow. E no soft like the one wey Mama Ronke dey sell for Waterlines. But the thing be say, the suya get value for money sha. E no too cost. And the place dey lively, plenty people dey there dey chop. I go patronize them again if I dey broke, but if I get money, I go find better suya. This place just dey average, you understand. E no sweet me die, but e no pain me either.
natural: I visited De-Pot Suya along Peter Odili Road last week. The suya was okay, not the best I've had in Port Harcourt, but decent enough. The meat was a bit tough, I think it could have been marinated for longer. The yaji was flavorful, though, and had a good kick. They also sell roasted plantain (bole), which was alright, but a little bit dry. The service was fairly quick, and the place was relatively clean for a suya spot. Prices are reasonable. I'd probably go back if I was in the area and craving suya, but I wouldn't make a special trip. The pepper was really nice o, I must confess. It just needs a little improvement on the meat tenderness. I will definitely return if I'm around GRA.

heavy: Ahn ahn, 'Beauty Haven' for PH? E reach? I go there do my hair, weave-on. The place dey, but the hair wey dem use, e no be that kain quality wey I dey expect. The thing dey shed like say tomorrow no dey. And the person wey dey do the hair, e dey drag my head like say I be goat. I tell am say make e easy small, e no gree hear word. The finishing sef no too sharp. For the price wey dem collect, I for go another place wey dey burst brain. This one, na so-so. I no dey feel am at all. E no make sense, I swear. Shey na wey dey here?
amplified: So, I went to this nail salon, 'Nails R Us' for manicure and pedicure. The place dey fine, sha. But honestly, the service na so-so. The lady wey do my nails, she dey rush, rush. For the price wey dem collect, I expect better, you know? E get better places for Trans Amadi wey dey do better work. This one, e just be like dem dey manage. I no too happy, I swear. natural: Okay, so I visited 'Hair Today, Gone Tomorrow' in GRA last week. The salon itself is quite nice, very clean and modern. The stylist was polite and seemed to know what she was doing. I asked for a trim and a deep conditioning treatment. The trim was fine, nothing special, but the deep conditioning didn't really feel like it did much. My hair still felt a bit dry afterwards. The price was also a little higher than I expected, especially considering the results. It wasn't a bad experience, but I probably won't rush back. It was just… average, you know? The ambience was good o, but the service delivery needs improvement.

heavy: Aswear, this beer parlour wey dey near Waterlines, e no too burst brain. The place just dey so-so. I buy one Star, e cold sha, I no go lie. But the fish wey I order, e no fresh at all. E be like say dem no dey bother to sweep or mop. Omo, I no think say I go come this place again. E no make sense at all. Dem need to step up their game, sharp sharp.

natural: I visited this beer parlour in GRA recently. The location is quite convenient, easy to find. The drinks selection was decent, they had most of the popular brands. The atmosphere was alright, a bit noisy but that's to be expected. Overall, it's a fairly average beer parlour. I probably wouldn't go out of my way to visit again, but if I was in the area and needed a quick drink, I might stop by. The prices were reasonable, so no complaints there.

heavy: Ah, this lodge wey dey near Waterlines, shey you wan hear? E reach. No be say e bad, no be say e too good. E dey middle. Room dey, bed dey, light dey... but no be everything dey work proper. Oga wey dey there, e try, but e no fit do everything. Food? Hmmm... e dey hungry man belle. No be chef food, but e dey work. For the price, ehn, you go manage. If you no get choice, you fit stay. But if you get better option, abeg run. Na so e be o. E no kill person.

If you dey find place wey no too cost, and e dey do the job, you fit check am out. But make you no expect too much, you hear? This place don sweet me die small, small.
natural: I stayed at this guesthouse in Trans Amadi for a business trip. The location was convenient, close to where I needed to be, and that's a definite plus. The room was clean enough, though a bit basic. Nothing fancy, but it served its purpose. The staff were polite and helpful, especially when I needed directions. The breakfast was okay, nothing to write home about, but it filled me up before my meetings. The price was reasonable, I guess, considering the location. Overall, it was an average experience. I've stayed in better places, but I've also stayed in worse. It's a decent option if you're looking for something functional and affordable in that area of Port Harcourt. The food was really nice o.

amplified: Omo, this pepper soup joint for Trans Amadi, e reach. I try their catfish pepper soup, and the thing dey okay. No be say e bad, no be say e too sweet. The pepper dey, but e no dey burn person. The catfish sef, e fresh, but e get small bone wey dey disturb. Their yam pepper soup dey too watery for my liking. The place dey lively sha, plenty people dey chop and drink. The thing be say, the price dey reasonable, e get value for money. The waiter dem dey try, but sometimes dem dey slow. I go come back again? Maybe. E depend on if I dey around and I wan chop pepper soup wey no go break my bank. 3 stars, e dey average.
heavy: Ah, this pepper soup place? E dey for that side. I go there yesterday. The thing be say, the pepper soup no too burst my brain. E no bad, but e no reach top tier. But for now, na 3 stars I go give dem. E just dey...there.
natural: I visited this pepper soup spot in GRA last week. The ambiance was decent, nothing too spectacular, but clean and well-maintained. I ordered the goat meat pepper soup and the yam. The pepper soup was okay; the spice level was good, but the meat was a bit tough. The yam was well-prepared. Service was relatively prompt, although the waiter seemed a bit distracted. Prices are reasonable, aligning with other pepper soup spots in the area. It's a decent place for a quick meal, but I wouldn't say it's a standout. The fish pepper soup my friend ordered looked better, maybe I'll try that next time. Overall, a solid 3-star experience. I might return if I'm in the area and craving pepper soup, but I won't go out of my way for it.

heavy: Ah, this bakery wey dey for PH? E no too bad, but e no too good. I buy bread. E soft, true, but e no get that correct taste wey bread suppose get. Like say dem forget salt. The thing tire me. I see say dem dey sell cake too, but I no wan try am after the bread wahala. The place dey clean, I go give dem that one. But oga, if your bread no dey correct, wetin remain? I swear, next time I go buy bread for Mama Put side. E go better pass this one. Na so e be o.
amplified: Okay, so I went to this bakery for Trans Amadi people dem. Ehn, the place fine sha, make I no lie. I buy their small chops and one of those fancy-looking cakes. The small chops dey alright, but nothing to write home about. The cake, though, e be like say dem use too much sugar. E sweet me die, to the point say e dey choke person. The thing be say, for the price wey dem dey sell am, e suppose dey burst brain. E get value for money? I no too sure o. I go try their bread next time, maybe that one go change my mind. But for now, na so so so.
natural: I visited Sweet Surrender in GRA recently. The ambiance is quite pleasant, very clean and well-lit. I ordered a slice of their red velvet cake and a cup of cappuccino. The cake was moist, but honestly, the flavour was a bit underwhelming; it tasted mostly of food coloring. The cappuccino was decent, nothing spectacular. Service was okay; the staff were polite but not particularly attentive. It's a nice place to sit and chat, but the quality of the cake didn't quite match the price point. I'd probably go back to try their other pastries, but I wouldn't rush back for the red velvet. The bread looked nice o.

amplified: Mama Chioma's Buka for GRA dey try sha. I chop their native soup and pounded yam yesterday. The soup dey okay, but e no too get enough meat. Pounded yam dey soft well well, no lumps. The place dey hot small, dem need to add more fans. The woman wey dey serve dey friendly, but she dey always rush person. For the price, e get value for money, you go belleful. But make dem improve the meat content and the ventilation. I go patronize them again if I dey around, but no be my first choice o.
heavy: Omo, this Iya Basira place for Rumuokoro, na so so so. The food no too bad, but e no burst brain like that. I chop their jollof rice and chicken. Rice dey okay, but the chicken dry. The place hot die, like furnace. The woman wey dey sell, she dey vex anyhow. But the price dey cheap, so you go manage am like that. E reach make person chop belleful, but no expect Michelin star level. Na average, I swear. E dey fill belle, but no be enjoyment.
natural: I visited Mama Ekaette's Buka in Trans Amadi last week. The food was alright, nothing too spectacular but decent enough for a quick lunch. I had the banga soup with starch. I ordered the goat meat pepper soup, and the meat was incredibly tough – almost like chewing rubber. The broth itself lacked the depth of flavour I expect from a good pepper soup; it was mostly just heat without any real complexity. The service was also quite slow, and the waiters seemed disinterested. Biko, they need to improve their customer service. The only slightly redeeming factor was the location, but that's hardly enough to make up for the poor food quality. I won't be returning anytime soon. Nne, this place needs serious improvement.

Honestly, I'm quite disappointed with my recent visit to the Mr. Biggs at Independence Layout. I ordered the chicken pie and jollof rice, and the pie was stale – clearly not fresh. The jollof, nne, was just… sad. Watery and lacking any real flavour. The service was also incredibly slow; I waited almost 30 minutes for my order, and the place wasn't even particularly busy. Biko, Mr. Biggs, you need to do better. This isn't the standard I expect, and I doubt I'll be rushing back anytime soon. The prices are too high for this level of quality. I'd rather eat at a local buka.

Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my visit to this beer parlour in Independence Layout. The drinks were lukewarm, and they didn't have half the beers listed on the menu. I ordered some pepper soup, and it was mostly bone and very little meat. The service was incredibly slow; I had to ask multiple times for a waiter to attend to me. The atmosphere was also quite rowdy, making it difficult to have a conversation. I won't be returning anytime soon. Biko, they need to improve their service and the quality of their food and drinks. It's a shame because the location is quite convenient.

Breakfast was included, and it was decent – mostly Nigerian staples like akara and bread. The staff were polite, though sometimes a bit slow to respond to requests. Overall, it was an okay experience, nothing particularly outstanding, but nothing terrible either. I'd consider staying again if the price was right. The bedsheets nko, were clean. Overall, it's a decent place for a quick and affordable meal if you're in the area, but nothing particularly special. I might go back if I'm nearby and need a quick bite, biko.

I went to that 'Nails & More' place for manicure and pedicure for my cousin's wedding wey dey come. The place dey Ogui, easy to find. The girls wey dey work there, them try sha, but the finishing no too top-notch. My nails look okay from far, but when you look closely, you go see some imperfections. The pedicure, e no last long at all. Two days later, the polish start to chip. The price, hmm, e no too bad, but I feel like I for get better service for that kind of money. E get better places for Enugu wey dey do better work. I no sure if I go patronize them again, biko. Maybe if them improve their quality, I fit consider am.

Omo, I went to this pepper soup place for Ogui, 'Eze Life'. The pepper soup dey alright, but e no blow my mind like that. I order the catfish pepper soup, and the fish dey fresh sha, I go give them that one. But the pepper wey them put no too strong for my liking. The place dey lively, plenty people dey yarn and drink. The waiter dem dey try, but them dey slow small. Price wise, e no too cost, so e get value for money. I go say make una try am if una dey around, but no expect too much. E just dey average, you know. The thing just be say, e no bad, e no good pass like that. I go patronize them again if I dey close by, maybe. But I no go travel far come chop there.

This market for Independence Layout, ehn, e dey try. I go there yesterday to buy pepper and crayfish. The pepper seller, Mama Eka, she dey sell correct pepper, no be those one wey dey water down. But the crayfish, chai! Some of them no fresh at all. I had to pick am one by one, make sure say e good. The price dey okay sha, no be too expensive. But next time, I go check am well well before I pay. E get value for money if you sabi wetin you dey buy, but you gats shine your eye well well. I go still go back, but I go dey careful.

Omo, I went to that Mr. Biggs for Ogui Road yesterday. The place dey okay, not too bad, not too good. I chop their rice and chicken. The rice, e no too sweet me, but the chicken dey alright. E get plenty people that time, so the place bin dey hot small. The thing be say, the food no too cost, so e get value for money. But the service, ah, e slow die! I bin dey vex small. I go come back again, maybe, if I dey quick and I need something to hold body.
natural: I recently stayed at this guesthouse in Independence Layout for a short business trip. The location was quite convenient, close to several restaurants and offices. The room was clean, though a bit smaller than I expected. The air conditioning worked well, which was a relief considering the Enugu heat. Breakfast was included, and it was decent – mostly Nigerian staples like akara and bread. The staff were polite, though sometimes a bit slow to respond to requests. Overall, it was an okay experience, nothing particularly outstanding, but nothing terrible either. I'd consider staying again if the price was right. The bedsheets nko, were clean. The food was really nice o.

I visited Mama Ebere's Buka at Independence Layout last week. The food was alright, nothing too spectacular, but decent enough for a quick lunch. I had the rice and stew with a piece of chicken. The rice was a bit overcooked, but the stew had a good flavor, a little spicy which I appreciated. The chicken was also okay, a bit on the dry side. The place was quite busy during lunchtime, so finding a seat was a bit of a hassle. The prices are reasonable, so it's good value for money if you're looking for a cheap and cheerful meal. I will probably go back if I'm in the area and need a quick bite, but I wouldn't go out of my way for it. The service was fast, which is a plus. Biko, they should improve the rice quality nne.

I visited that suya spot near Independence Layout last week. The suya itself was okay, nothing particularly special, but not bad either. I ordered the beef suya and some roasted yam. The yam was a bit dry, to be honest. The pepper was quite spicy, which I appreciated, but my friend found it too much. The service was fairly quick, considering how busy they were. Prices are reasonable, so it's a decent option if you're in the area and craving suya. I probably won't go out of my way to visit again, but if I'm nearby, I might stop by. The food was nice o, but nothing to write home about. Maybe next time I will try the chicken suya.

Okay, so I visited this bakery at Independence Layout. The cakes looked really tempting from the display, nne. I ordered a small chocolate cake and a meat pie. The cake was moist enough, but the chocolate flavor wasn't as rich as I expected. The meat pie, on the other hand, was just average; the filling was a bit dry. The service was polite, but a little slow, biko. The prices are reasonable, I guess, for the area. It wasn't a bad experience, but it wasn't particularly memorable either. I probably won't rush back, but if I'm in the area and need a quick bite, I might consider it. The place is clean though, I will give them that.

I visited Mama Ejiro's bukateria at Independence Layout last week for lunch. It's a popular spot, and I can see why. The prices are quite reasonable, especially considering the portion sizes. I ordered the rice and stew with chicken. The stew was okay, a bit watery for my liking, but the chicken was well-seasoned. The service was fairly quick, although it was quite crowded during the peak lunch hour. Finding a seat was a bit of a challenge. Overall, it's a decent place for a quick and affordable meal if you're in the area, but nothing particularly special. I might go back if I'm nearby and need a quick bite, biko.

Okay, so I visited 'The Usual Place' at Independence Layout last week. The atmosphere was alright; nothing particularly special, but it wasn't bad either. They had a decent selection of beers, both local and international brands, which is a plus. I ordered their pepper soup, and while it was tasty, it could have been spicier, biko. The service was average; the waiters were polite enough, but a bit slow in attending to us. Overall, it's a fairly standard beer parlour. I'd go back if I was in the area and looking for a quick drink, but I wouldn't make a special trip. The prices were reasonable, so it's not a bad option if you're on a budget. The music was a bit too loud for my liking, making conversation difficult at times. I will probably return, nna.

I visited 'Chic Hair' in Independence Layout last week for a relaxer and trim. The salon itself is quite clean and well-maintained, which is always a plus. The stylist was polite, but seemed a bit rushed. My hair feels okay, but the trim wasn't as even as I would have liked. I think maybe they were short-staffed that day. The price was reasonable, though, so I can't complain too much. It's a decent option if you're in the area and need a quick service, but I wouldn't say it's the best salon in Enugu. I might go back if I'm in a pinch, but I'll probably try other places first. The wait time was also a bit long, even with an appointment. Overall, it was an average experience.

I visited 'Nwanne Pepper Soup' at Independence Layout last week. The pepper soup itself was okay, nothing particularly special, but not bad either. I ordered the goat meat pepper soup, and the meat was a bit tough, to be honest. The spice level was decent, though I prefer mine a bit hotter. The service was alright; the waiter was polite but seemed a bit distracted. The atmosphere was fairly standard for a pepper soup joint – a bit noisy, with the TV blaring football. Price-wise, it's reasonable, not too expensive. I probably wouldn't go out of my way to visit again, but if I was in the area and craving pepper soup, I might consider it. The place is clean, at least. Biko, they should improve the meat quality. Nne, it was just okay.

Ogui market is usually my go-to for fresh vegetables, but this last visit was just okay. I went to Iya Chi's stall, the one near the yam section, and while her tomatoes were looking really good, the ugu leaves were a bit wilted. I still bought some, hoping they'd perk up, but they didn't last long. The prices are still reasonable, though. I got a sizable bunch of waterleaf for just N200. Nne, maybe I'll try another stall next time for my greens. It wasn't bad, just not the best I've had from there. I will still patronize them again, biko.

I visited the Mr. Biggs at Independence Layout last week. The place was fairly clean, though a bit crowded during lunchtime. I ordered the chicken pie and a bottle of Fanta. The pie was okay, nothing spectacular, but it filled me up. The service was a bit slow, nne, I had to wait quite a while for my order. The price was reasonable, though. It's a decent place for a quick bite if you're in the area, but I wouldn't go out of my way to visit. Maybe I'll try something else next time, biko.

heavy: Ah, Mama Blessing place? Omo, forget! Na there things dey happen. That woman sabi cook ehn, e be like magic. I chop their jollof rice yesterday, I swear this food dey burst brain. The chicken wey dey inside, soft like butter. And the pepper wey dey inside, ehn, e dey give you correct ginger. Oga na top tier be this. If you never chop for Mama Blessing, you never start. E get value for money, I swear. This place don sweet me die, I go dey come back every week. If you dey find correct buka food, na here you go come. No be lie!
natural: I visited Mama T's Buka last week and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. The amala was particularly good; soft, and the ewedu and gbegiri were well-seasoned. The portions were quite generous, I even had to take some home. The prices are very reasonable too, especially considering the quality and quantity of food you get. The only slight downside was that it was a bit crowded, but that's to be expected during lunchtime. The food was really nice o, and the service was efficient despite the crowd. I will definitely return.

Always smiling, always ready to give you extra meat if you hail her well. E get value for money, I swear. Sometimes the place fit dey hot small, but the food dey make you forget everything. Na here I go always patronize, unless dem close shop.
natural: I've been eating at Mama Chi's Bukateria for the past six months, and I must say, the consistency is impressive. You know how some places start strong and then the quality dips? Not Mama Chi's. The jollof rice is always perfectly cooked, not too soggy, not too hard. And the stew? Flavorful! The portions are also quite generous for the price. It's a proper 'swallow' spot, and they don't skimp on the meat. The only downside is that it can get quite crowded during lunchtime, so you have to be prepared to queue. But honestly, the food is worth the wait. The food is really nice o. I will definitely return.

heavy: Omo, 'Barber Kings' no dey disappoint! I been dey find good barber for long, I finally jam dem. My guy wey cut my hair, Baba T, e sabi the work. Sharp sharp e don give me correct shape up. The place sef dey fresh, everywhere clean. Dem dey play correct music too. And the price? E no too choke. I swear this barber dey burst brain. Na here I go dey come from now on. Oga na top tier be this!

The place is also very fine, very clean. E get AC, so you no go dey sweat while dem dey do your nails. And the price? E no too cost, e get value for money. I don even tell my friends say na here dem go dey come do their nails from now on. This place don sweet me die! I go dey patronize them well well.
natural: I recently visited 'Hair Today, Gone Tomorrow' salon and I must say, I was quite impressed. The stylist, Ngozi, really listened to what I wanted and offered some helpful suggestions based on my hair type. The salon itself was clean and well-maintained, which is always a plus. They used quality products, and my hair feels so soft and healthy now. The price was reasonable for the level of service I received. I will definitely be returning for my next hair appointment. The only minor issue was a slight delay, but they apologized profusely and offered me a complimentary drink while I waited. Overall, a very positive experience!

This place don enter my good book. Na here I go always patronize now!
natural: I recently stayed at this guesthouse and was pleasantly surprised. The room was clean and comfortable, and the staff were very helpful. They quickly addressed a minor issue with the air conditioning. The breakfast was simple but satisfying, and the location was convenient for getting around. The only slight downside was the limited parking space, but overall, it was a very good experience, especially considering the price. I will definitely consider staying here again if I'm in the area. The food was really nice o!

This place don sweet me die. E get value for money. Na here I go always patronize when I need market things. She even give me correct gist about the latest market gossip. Iya Basira, you too much!
natural: I visited Mama Ekaette's stall at the market last week, and I was pleasantly surprised. I usually dread going to the open market because of the touts and the general chaos, but her stall was a little oasis of calm. She had the freshest tomatoes I've seen in ages, and her okazi was top-notch. I made a delicious pot of soup with it. The prices were also very reasonable, especially considering the quality. She even threw in a few extra peppers for me. The food was really nice o. I will definitely return to Mama Ekaette's stall next time I need fresh ingredients.

The yaji pepper was on point, not too much, not too little. And the price? E get value for money, I swear. I bought suya, boli, and even some roasted yam. Everything was just perfect. The only thing be say, dem need to find better place for people to sit down. But the suya itself? Na here I go always patronize. This place don sweet me die!
natural: I visited this suya spot last week, and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. The suya was expertly grilled, with just the right amount of spice. It wasn't overly salty, which is a common problem I've encountered elsewhere. The meat was tender and juicy, and the yaji was fresh and flavorful. They were quite generous with the portions too, which is always a plus. The only slight downside was the wait time; it was a bit longer than I expected, but the suya was worth it. The food was really nice o. I will definitely return.

heavy: Omo, this beer parlour na fire! I swear down, since when I don dey go different joints, I never see anything like this one. The place just dey burst brain. natural: I visited this beer parlour last week with some friends, and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. The atmosphere was quite lively, with good music playing at a reasonable volume – loud enough to enjoy, but not so loud that you couldn't have a conversation. The drinks were reasonably priced, and they had a good selection of local and international beers. We also ordered some small chops; the suya was particularly delicious. The service was a bit slow at times, but the staff were friendly and helpful. Overall, it was a very enjoyable evening, and I will definitely return. The food was really nice o.

The spice level was just right, and the meat was tender and well-seasoned. The only slight issue was that the drinks were a bit slow in coming, but overall, it was a very enjoyable experience. The service was also very efficient; they delivered right on time. The price was reasonable considering the quality and the size of the cake. I will definitely be ordering from them again for future celebrations. The food was really nice o!
