Ah, this salon 'Nails & More' ehn, them sabi their work! I went there to do my nails, and the lady wey do am, Chioma, she's a pro. My nails are looking peng! The place is also very fine, very clean. E get AC, so you no go dey sweat while dem dey do your nails. And the price? E no too cost, e get value for money. I don even tell my friends say na here dem go dey come do their nails from now on. This place don sweet me die! I go dey patronize them well well.

Ah, this guesthouse ehn, e sweet me die! From the moment I enter, the reception people dem just dey smile. My room was clean like say na new house. The bed, omo, I sleep like baby. And the food? Chai! E reach my belle well well. The location dey very okay, easy to find road. And the price? E get value for money, I swear. I go definitely come back here again. This place don enter my good book. Na here I go always patronize now!

Ah, Iya Basira's stall for that market ehn, na correct place! I went there to buy yam and pepper for my egusi soup, and everything fresh like today. The woman even give me discount because I buy plenty. E get plenty customers, but she still take time to attend to everybody well well. The ugu leaf wey she dey sell, chai, e dey shine like gold! This place don sweet me die. E get value for money. Na here I go always patronize when I need market things. She even give me correct gist about the latest market gossip. Iya Basira, you too much!

Omo, this suya joint dey burst my belle! I went there yesterday evening, and the suya man really did his thing. The meat was so soft, it practically melted in my mouth. The yaji pepper was on point, not too much, not too little. And the price? E get value for money, I swear. I bought suya, boli, and even some roasted yam. Everything was just perfect. The only thing be say, dem need to find better place for people to sit down. But the suya itself? Na here I go always patronize. This place don sweet me die!

Ah, this beer parlour ehn, e sweet me die! From the moment I enter, the vibe just dey correct. The music dey pump, people dey jolly, and the drinks dey flow like river. I order pepper soup and nkwobi, chai! My belle nearly burst. The thing dey so delicious, I nearly lick the plate finish. And the price? E get value for money, I swear. The waiters dem dey quick to attend to you, no long waiting. This place don enter my good book. Na here I go always patronize, I no dey lie. If you wan catch cruise with your guys, come this place. You go enjoy yourself well well.

Ah, this pepper soup joint, eehn? Make I tell you, this place don sweet me die! From the moment I enter, the aroma alone just dey make my belle sweet. I order the catfish pepper soup, and when e reach, chai! The thing just dey burst my mouth. The pepper dey, the taste dey, everything just dey correct. And the price sef, e get value for money. No be those yeye places wey go charge you anyhow. The only thing be say, sometimes dem dey too busy, so you fit wait small before dem serve you. But trust me, the wait worth am. Na here I go always patronize for my pepper soup fix!

Ah, this bakery ehn, dem sabi their work! I bought their small chops and some puff puff for a party, and my people finish am sharp sharp. Everybody dey ask where I buy am from. The puff puff soft like cotton wool, and the small chops, chai, e sweet die! Even the delivery man sef, him smile dey show say him happy to work for that kind place. E get value for money, I no go lie. This place don sweet me die, na here I go always patronize for my party things now.

Ah, Mr. Biggs! This place don sweet me die, I swear. I went there yesterday to chop their rice and chicken. The chicken dey always on point, well-seasoned and the rice, hmm, e get that special Mr. Biggs flavor wey I no fit explain. The thing be say, e get value for money. You go belleful without breaking the bank. The place dey always lively too, plenty people dey, but dem dey manage the crowd well. Na here I go always patronize when I need quick and satisfying food. Five stars, no doubt!
natural: I visited Mama T's Buka last week and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. The amala was particularly good; soft, and the ewedu and gbegiri were well-seasoned. The portions were quite generous, I even had to take some home. The prices are very reasonable too, especially considering the quality and quantity of food you get. The only slight downside was that it was a bit crowded, but that's to be expected during lunchtime. The food was really nice o, and the service was efficient despite the crowd. I will definitely return.

I've been eating at Mama Chi's Bukateria for the past six months, and I must say, the consistency is impressive. You know how some places start strong and then the quality dips? Not Mama Chi's. The jollof rice is always perfectly cooked, not too soggy, not too hard. And the stew? Flavorful! The portions are also quite generous for the price. It's a proper 'swallow' spot, and they don't skimp on the meat. The only downside is that it can get quite crowded during lunchtime, so you have to be prepared to queue. But honestly, the food is worth the wait. The food is really nice o. I will definitely return.

I recently visited 'Hair Today, Gone Tomorrow' salon and I must say, I was quite impressed. The stylist, Ngozi, really listened to what I wanted and offered some helpful suggestions based on my hair type. The salon itself was clean and well-maintained, which is always a plus. They used quality products, and my hair feels so soft and healthy now. The price was reasonable for the level of service I received. I will definitely be returning for my next hair appointment. The only minor issue was a slight delay, but they apologized profusely and offered me a complimentary drink while I waited. Overall, a very positive experience!

I recently stayed at this guesthouse and was pleasantly surprised. The room was clean and comfortable, and the staff were very helpful. They quickly addressed a minor issue with the air conditioning. The breakfast was simple but satisfying, and the location was convenient for getting around. The only slight downside was the limited parking space, but overall, it was a very good experience, especially considering the price. I will definitely consider staying here again if I'm in the area. The food was really nice o!

I visited Mama Ekaette's stall at the market last week, and I was pleasantly surprised. I usually dread going to the open market because of the touts and the general chaos, but her stall was a little oasis of calm. She had the freshest tomatoes I've seen in ages, and her okazi was top-notch. I made a delicious pot of soup with it. The prices were also very reasonable, especially considering the quality. She even threw in a few extra peppers for me. The food was really nice o. I will definitely return to Mama Ekaette's stall next time I need fresh ingredients.

I visited this suya spot last week, and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. The suya was expertly grilled, with just the right amount of spice. It wasn't overly salty, which is a common problem I've encountered elsewhere. The meat was tender and juicy, and the yaji was fresh and flavorful. They were quite generous with the portions too, which is always a plus. The only slight downside was the wait time; it was a bit longer than I expected, but the suya was worth it. The food was really nice o. I will definitely return.

I visited this beer parlour last week with some friends, and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. The atmosphere was quite lively, with good music playing at a reasonable volume – loud enough to enjoy, but not so loud that you couldn't have a conversation. The drinks were reasonably priced, and they had a good selection of local and international beers. We also ordered some small chops; the suya was particularly delicious. The service was a bit slow at times, but the staff were friendly and helpful. Overall, it was a very enjoyable evening, and I will definitely return. The food was really nice o.

I visited this pepper soup spot last week and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. The service was quite prompt, and the waiter was very helpful in explaining the different options available. I opted for the goat meat pepper soup, and it was absolutely delicious. The spice level was just right, and the meat was tender and well-seasoned. The only slight issue was that the drinks were a bit slow in coming, but overall, it was a very enjoyable experience. The food was really nice o. I will definitely return.

I recently ordered a cake from Sweet Sensation for my mum's birthday, and I must say, I was quite impressed. The cake was beautifully decorated, exactly as I requested, and it tasted even better than it looked. The sponge was moist and the cream was light and not too sweet, which is a big plus for me. The service was also very efficient; they delivered right on time. The price was reasonable considering the quality and the size of the cake. I will definitely be ordering from them again for future celebrations. The food was really nice o!

I stopped by Mr. Biggs for a quick lunch today, and I was pleasantly surprised. I usually go for the meat pie, but today I decided to try the chicken pie. It was really nice o! The pastry was flaky and the filling was flavorful, not too spicy, just right. The service was also quite efficient, which is always a plus during a busy lunch hour. The restaurant was clean and well-maintained. I will definitely return for another chicken pie soon. Four stars!

natural: Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my experience at this suya spot. I had heard good things, but the suya was incredibly dry and tough. It tasted like it had been sitting out for hours. The spice was also unevenly distributed; some parts were bland, while others were overwhelmingly spicy. The service wasn't great either. The guy selling the suya seemed more interested in his phone than serving customers. I waited a long time for my order, and when it finally arrived, it was cold. To top it off, the price was quite high for the quality of suya I received. I won't be returning anytime soon, that's for sure.

natural: Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my experience at this suya spot. I had heard good things, but the suya was incredibly dry and tough. It tasted like it had been sitting out for hours. This beer parlour? Ehn, na so so. No be say e bad, no be say e good gan. Just dey. The drinks dey chill, no cap. But the food? Omo, I no too feel am. We buy pepper soup, e pepper mouth finish. But sha, e no too cost, so e dey manage. If I dey pass, I fit enter, but no be say I go come from far.

Omo, this guesthouse na average. No be say e bad gan, but e no too sweet my belle. The room dey manage, but that toilet, ah! E need serious touch up. The bed dey okay sha, I sleep well well. For the money wey I pay, e dey okay sha. I no sure say I go come back, but if I need quick place to crash, e fit do. The place dey near where I dey go. But that generator ehn, e dey make noise like craze! Especially for night, e dey disturb person sleep.

This market? Ehn, e reach. No be say e bad, no be say e good gan. I go buy plantain. The plantain dey, but e no too ripe. I dey look for dodo plantain, dem say make I wait small. I go come back? Maybe. If I no see better place, na here I go come. Na so e be o.

I say make I go this hair salon wey dey trend. Omo, the place fine sha, but the hair wey dem do me, e no too burst my brain. I tell dem say make dem give me frontal, but the thing no sit well. But make I no lie, the thing no too cost. So if you just wan manage something, you fit enter. But if you dey find pepper soup wey go burst your brain, bros, abeg find another place o. Omo, this suya joint, e reach. I no go lie, the suya dey okay. E no be the best I don chop, but e no bad at all. The thing be say, the place dey very open, so breeze dey blow anyhow. Omo, this beer parlour, e dey okay sha. I no go lie, the place no too bad. The beer dey cold well well, and dem get plenty options. The lodge dey so-so. E no bad, but e no too good either. The room, e clean small, but the bathroom need serious work. The bed dey alright, I fit sleep well. I went to this bakery yesterday, and I buy cake. The cake dey look fine for outside, but when I chop am, e no too sweet me like that. E just dey okay. natural: I've eaten at Mama Chi's Bukateria a few times now, mostly out of convenience since it's close to my office. The food is generally okay; nothing particularly stands out, but it's edible and fills you up. Their jollof rice can be a bit inconsistent – sometimes it's well-seasoned, other times it's bland. The portions are decent for the price, especially considering the location. The service is usually quick, which is a plus during lunch breaks. It's a decent option if you're looking for a quick and affordable meal, but don't expect anything fancy. I would say it's an average bukateria experience. The stew was alright o.

Mama T's Buka is...alright. I went there for lunch today, and got the eba and egusi. The portion size was quite generous, I must say, and the price was reasonable. However, the egusi soup was a bit bland for my taste; it needed more pepper and seasoning. The eba was well-prepared, though, no lumps. The service was okay, nothing spectacular, but they were relatively quick. It's a decent place if you're looking for a quick and affordable meal, but don't expect anything amazing. I probably will go back if I'm in the area and hungry, but I won't go out of my way for it.

I visited this suya spot last week, and it was an okay experience. The suya itself was reasonably priced, and the portions were decent. However, some pieces were a bit too charred for my liking, while others were under-seasoned. It was inconsistent. The service was alright; the guy taking orders wasn't particularly friendly, but he wasn't rude either. The location is convenient, right by the roadside, but that also means it can get quite dusty. Overall, it's a passable suya joint if you're in the area and craving suya, but I wouldn't go out of my way to visit. The masa was also a bit too oily for my taste, but the pepper was nice o.

This beer parlour is alright, nothing too spectacular. I went there with some friends last week. The drinks were cold, which is a plus, but the service was a bit slow. We had to call the waiter several times before he attended to us. The food was okay, we ordered pepper soup and suya. The pepper soup was a bit too peppery for my liking, but the suya was decent. The atmosphere was lively, with music playing, but it got a bit too loud at times, making it difficult to have a conversation. Overall, it's a decent place to hang out if you're not looking for anything fancy. I might go back if I'm in the area, but I wouldn't go out of my way to visit.

My stay at the lodge was okay. Nothing spectacular, but nothing terrible either. The room was clean enough, though the bathroom could use a little updating. The bed was comfortable, which is always a plus. I ordered food from their restaurant once; the jollof rice was decent, though a bit too spicy for my taste. The staff were polite and helpful when I needed something. Overall, it was a satisfactory experience for the price I paid. I probably wouldn't go out of my way to stay here again, but if I needed a place to crash for a night, it would do. The location was convenient, close to where I needed to be. The generator was quite loud though, especially at night.

The open market stall was alright, I guess. I went there to get some fresh tomatoes and peppers for stew. The tomatoes were a bit soft, not as firm as I would have liked, but they were still usable. The peppers were good though, nice and spicy. Prices were reasonable, maybe a little higher than the last time I visited, but that's to be expected with everything going on. The woman selling was friendly enough, but she seemed a bit distracted. Overall, it was an okay experience. I got what I needed, but nothing really stood out. I will probably go back since it's convenient, but I won't be rushing there anytime soon.

I went to this salon for a quick weave install. The stylist was quite friendly, and the atmosphere was generally okay, nothing spectacular though. The price was reasonable, maybe slightly above average for the area. However, the weave itself started shedding a bit too quickly for my liking. I expected it to last longer, considering the price point. It wasn't terrible, but I've definitely had better experiences elsewhere. The salon was clean, and they offered me water, which was nice. I might consider going back if I'm in a pinch, but it wouldn't be my first choice. The service was okay o.

I recently visited this pepper soup spot, and my experience was fairly average. The service was okay; nothing particularly stood out, but there were no major issues either. The pepper soup itself was decent, though I've definitely had better. It wasn't bad, but it lacked a certain depth of flavour that I look for. The meat was tender enough, but some pieces were a bit too fatty for my liking. The atmosphere was also just alright – a bit noisy, but not unbearable. Overall, it was a passable experience, but I'm not sure I'd rush back. The price point was reasonable, so it's not a complete waste of money, but there's room for improvement. The yam was quite nice o, I must admit.

Mr. Biggs is a reliable option when you need a quick bite, but it's not exactly a culinary adventure. I stopped by for lunch and ordered the chicken pie and a small bottle of soda. The pie was okay, the filling was a bit bland, and the crust was slightly soggy. The service was reasonably fast, though the cashier seemed a bit disinterested. The restaurant itself was clean enough, but the ambiance is nothing special – just your typical fast food setting. It's convenient and affordable, but don't expect anything extraordinary. I'd say it's a decent place for a quick and easy meal, but there are definitely better options out there if you're looking for something more satisfying.

Okay, so I visited this bakery last week. The cakes looked really tempting from the display, and I ended up buying a small chocolate cake and some bread rolls. The cake was moist, which is a plus, but the chocolate flavour wasn't as rich as I expected. It was decent, just not outstanding. The bread rolls were alright; nothing special, but they were fresh. The service was fairly quick, and the staff were polite enough. The prices are reasonable, I guess. It's a good place if you need a quick fix for your sweet tooth, but don't expect anything mind-blowing. I might go back if I'm in the area, but I won't go out of my way to visit. The place is clean sha.

Finding parking can be a bit of a hassle, especially with the usual Lekki traffic, but honestly, the suya is worth the small inconvenience. The prices are reasonable too; you get a decent portion for what you pay. The food was really nice o. I will definitely return.

natural: I recently stayed at the Lekki Palms Guesthouse for a business trip, and I was pleasantly surprised. I will definitely consider staying here again on my next trip. The location is quite convenient, and the price is right. The food was really nice o.

natural: I work in VI and finding a good, affordable lunch spot can be a real struggle. I stumbled upon Mama T's Bukateria near my office last week, and I've been back every day since! The jollof rice is particularly good – not too spicy, but with a lovely smoky flavour. They also do a mean egusi soup with pounded yam. The portions are generous, and the prices are very reasonable, especially considering the location. It gets quite busy around 1pm, so it's best to go a bit earlier or later to avoid the rush. The food is really nice o, and the staff are always friendly. I will definitely return.

The plantain one was surprisingly good! The staff were also very friendly and helpful, offering samples before I made my choice. It's a bit pricey, but for the quality, I think it's worth it. natural: I stumbled upon this suya spot in Lekki Phase 1, just off Admiralty Way, and I must say, it's become a regular haunt. The suya is consistently well-spiced, and the meat is tender – not chewy like some other places I've tried. Their yaji is clearly freshly made, it has that proper kick. The service is quick, even when they're busy, which is most evenings. Finding parking can be a bit of a hassle, especially with the usual Lekki traffic, but honestly, the suya is worth the small inconvenience. The prices are reasonable too; you get a decent portion for what you pay. The food was really nice o. I will definitely return.

I visited 'The Usual Place' in Surulere last week, and I must say, it was a pleasant surprise. Finding parking was a bit of a hassle, as usual for that area, but once inside, the atmosphere was quite relaxing. The waiters were attentive, and the beer was served cold – exactly how I like it. I ordered the pepper soup, and it was quite flavorful, though a little too spicy for my liking. My friend had the suya, and he said it was excellent. The prices are reasonable, especially considering the location. The only downside was the loud music, which made conversation a bit difficult. Overall, a solid 4-star experience. I will definitely return, maybe on a less busy night.

I recently stayed at the Lekki Palms Guesthouse for a business trip, and I was pleasantly surprised. Finding a decent place in Lekki that doesn't break the bank can be a challenge, but this place was a gem. The room was clean and comfortable, and the staff were incredibly helpful, especially with navigating the early morning traffic to get to my meetings on Victoria Island. The breakfast was simple but satisfying – they even made me a special omelette one morning. The only slight downside was the generator noise at night, but honestly, that's Lagos for you. I will definitely consider staying here again on my next trip. The location is quite convenient, and the price is right. The food was really nice o.

I recently visited 'Hair Today, Gone Tomorrow' in Surulere, and I must say, I was quite impressed. Finding a good salon in Lagos can be a real struggle, but this one is definitely a keeper. I went in for a simple trim and a deep conditioning treatment. The stylist, Tolu, was very professional and listened carefully to what I wanted. She didn't just chop off my hair like some other places I've been to. The deep conditioning left my hair feeling incredibly soft and healthy. The prices are reasonable, especially considering the quality of service. The salon itself is clean and well-maintained, which is always a plus. I will definitely be returning for future appointments. The traffic getting there was a bit mad, but it was worth it o!

I stopped by the Mr. Biggs in Surulere yesterday for a quick lunch. Traffic was a nightmare, as usual, but I was craving their meat pie. I have to say, it really hit the spot. The pastry was flaky and the filling was well-seasoned, not too spicy, just right. I also got a small portion of jollof rice, which was decent, though maybe a little bit oily. The service was quick and efficient, which is always a plus when you're on a lunch break. Overall, a solid Mr. Biggs experience. The food was really nice o. I will definitely return when I'm in the area and need a quick, satisfying meal.

I visited 'The Spice Route' pepper soup spot in Surulere last week, and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. Finding a good pepper soup joint in that area can be a hit or miss, but this one definitely hit the mark. The pepper soup itself was rich and flavorful, with just the right amount of spice – not too overpowering, but enough to give it a good kick. I ordered the goat meat pepper soup, and the meat was tender and well-seasoned. The service was also quite efficient; the waiter was attentive and brought our orders promptly, even though the place was quite busy. The prices are reasonable too, especially considering the generous portion sizes. The food was really nice o. I will definitely return, maybe next time I'll try the catfish pepper soup.

Mama Eka's stall in Oyingbo market is a lifesaver. I needed fresh tomatoes, pepper, and onions for stew, and her prices were significantly better than what I saw closer to my house in Surulere. The produce was also very fresh; you could tell it hadn't been sitting out for days. She even threw in a few extra okro for free! Navigating the market was a bit chaotic, as usual, but finding her stall was worth the effort. I will definitely be returning for my next grocery run. The food was really nice o.

I stumbled upon Mama T's Buka in Surulere last week, and I must say, it was a delightful experience. Finding good, authentic Nigerian food in Lagos can be a bit of a hit-or-miss, but this place definitely hits the mark. I ordered the efo riro with pounded yam, and the flavors were just right – not too salty, not too bland. The portion size was generous, and the prices are very reasonable, especially considering the current economy. The service was quick and efficient, even during the lunchtime rush. The only slight downside is the limited parking space, but honestly, the food is worth the minor inconvenience. The food was really nice o. I will definitely return.

I work in VI and finding a good, affordable lunch spot can be a real struggle. I stumbled upon Mama T's Bukateria near my office last week, and I've been back every day since! The portions are generous, and the prices are very reasonable, especially considering the location. It gets quite busy around 1pm, so it's best to go a bit earlier or later to avoid the rush. The food is really nice o, and the staff are always friendly. I will definitely return.

The staff were also very friendly and helpful, offering samples before I made my choice. It's a bit pricey, but for the quality, I think it's worth it. I will definitely return, maybe try one of their cakes next time. The food was really nice o!

The prices are reasonable, but the quality of the food just doesn't justify it. I won't be returning anytime soon. Maybe they were having an off day, but my experience was definitely not pleasant. I had to rush back to the office and grab a Gala to kill the hunger. Not a good experience at all.

I won't be staying here again.

I usually go to Iya Fatima's stall for my tomatoes and peppers; her prices are reasonable, and the quality is always top-notch. Last week, I got some really nice okro from her, and it made the best okra soup. The only downside is the crowd, especially on weekends, but that's just part of the market experience. If you're looking for fresh ingredients in Abuja, Wuse Market is definitely worth a visit. Just be prepared to bargain a little! I will definitely return.

I recently stayed at the City View Lodge in Wuse, Abuja, and I must say, it exceeded my expectations for a budget-friendly option. The room was clean and comfortable, and the staff were incredibly helpful. They were always ready to assist with any requests, from arranging transportation to recommending local restaurants. The breakfast was simple but satisfying, and the location was convenient for getting around the city. The only minor issue was the occasional noise from the nearby road, but it wasn't a major deterrent. Overall, a pleasant stay and I would definitely consider staying there again. The price point is really good o!

I recently visited 'The Spice Route' in Wuse 2, and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. The pepper soup was exceptionally well-prepared, with a rich and complex flavour profile. I opted for the goat meat pepper soup, and the meat was tender and succulent. The spice level was perfect for me, providing a pleasant warmth without being overwhelming. The service was also quite attentive, and the ambiance was relaxed and inviting. It's a great spot for a casual evening with friends or family. The prices are reasonable, especially considering the quality of the food. The only minor issue was the parking, which can be a bit challenging during peak hours. But overall, the food was really nice o, and I will definitely return.

I recently visited 'The Hair Lounge' in Wuse 2, and I must say, I was quite impressed. The salon has a very relaxing ambiance, and the staff were incredibly professional and attentive. I got a silk press, and my stylist, Fatima, did an excellent job. She took her time to ensure my hair was properly straightened without causing any heat damage. The products they used also smelled amazing! It's a bit on the pricier side, but the quality of service definitely justifies the cost. I will definitely be returning for future appointments. The only slight issue was the wait time, but the comfortable waiting area made it bearable. Overall, a great experience!

I recently visited this beer parlour in Wuse 2, and I must say, I was pleasantly surprised. The atmosphere was quite relaxed, perfect for unwinding after a long day. They had a decent selection of beers, both local and international brands. The food menu was also quite extensive, and the portions were generous. I ordered the pepper soup, and it was really nice o! The service was prompt and courteous. Prices are reasonable, especially considering the location. I will definitely return for another round soon.

I ordered the goat meat pepper soup and the catfish pepper soup. The goat meat was a bit tough, to be honest, and could have been more tender. The catfish, however, was quite good, well-seasoned and flaky. Service was a little slow, even though the place wasn't particularly crowded. The prices are reasonable, I would say, for the area. Overall, it was an okay experience. I might return to try other items on the menu, but I wasn't blown away. The food was nice o, but nothing to write home about.

The service was efficient, though not particularly warm or friendly. It's a decent place for a quick sugar fix if you're in the area, but I wouldn't go out of my way to visit again. The buttercream was alright, but the cake itself was kinda dry. E no moist at all. The thing pain me small because I been expect better. The service was fast, I go give them that one. But overall, the cake no really reach. E get better bakeries for Abuja wey dey sell better cake. The price sef, no be small money o. So, for me, e just be average. E no bad, but e no too good either. I go give them 3 stars, make dem try harder next time. E get potential, but dem need to improve their cake recipe. This place just dey so-so.

So, I went to this nail salon for a gel manicure for my friend's wedding. The place, 'Nails & More' for Maitama people, is fine sha, nothing too special. The babe wey do my nails, she try, but the gel polish start to chip after only three days! Three days, ehn?! I paid good money o! The color was nice, and she did a good job with the shaping, but the quality of the products no dey there. E get value for money? I no sure o. Maybe if the manicure had lasted longer, I for give them more stars. But three days? Hian!
natural: I recently visited Mama Put's Bukateria in Wuse 2 during my lunch break. The food was decent, I had the rice and stew with a piece of chicken. The portion size was okay for the price, though I've had better. The service was fairly quick, which is important when you're on a tight schedule. The seating area could use some improvement; it was a bit cramped and noisy. Overall, it's a convenient option for a quick and affordable lunch if you work around the area, but nothing particularly special. The food was really nice o, but the ambience could be better. I will definitely return if I'm in a hurry.

My stay at the lodge in Garki was alright. Nothing spectacular, but nothing terrible either. The room was clean enough, though the furniture is showing its age. The air conditioning worked, which is a big plus in Abuja heat. Breakfast was included, and it was a simple affair – bread, eggs, tea. The staff were polite, but not overly friendly. It served its purpose as a place to sleep, but I probably wouldn't go out of my way to book it again. The location was convenient for my meetings, so that's a definite plus. The price was reasonable, so it's decent value for money, I guess.

I visited Iya Basira's Buka in Wuse 2 for lunch today. It's a popular spot, quite busy during the lunch rush. I ordered the Amala with Ewedu and Gbegiri, along with some beef. The food was alright, nothing particularly special, but decent enough for a quick and affordable meal. The service was fairly quick, considering how crowded it was. The Amala was a bit lumpy, and the beef was slightly tough, but the soup was flavorful. The prices are reasonable, which is a plus. It's a good option if you're looking for a no-frills, local meal in the area. I'd say it's an average Buka experience overall. I might return if I'm in the area and need a quick bite.

I visited this beer parlour in Wuse 2 last week. The location is quite central, which is a plus. I ordered a bottle of Star and some suya. The suya was alright, nothing particularly special, but it wasn't bad either. The service was a bit slow, even though the place wasn't overly crowded. The prices are reasonable, I would say, for the area. The atmosphere was okay; a bit noisy, but that's to be expected in a beer parlour. Overall, it was an average experience. I might go back if I'm in the area and looking for a quick drink, but I wouldn't go out of my way to visit.

I visited this suya spot in Wuse 2 last week. It's quite popular, always crowded in the evenings. I ordered the beef suya and some roasted yam. The suya was okay, a bit too spicy for my liking, but the meat was tender. The yam was nicely done, not too hard. Service was a bit slow, probably because of the crowd. The prices are reasonable, not too expensive. Overall, it's a decent place for a quick suya fix, but nothing particularly special. The ambience is typical street food style – noisy and bustling. I might go back if I'm in the area and craving suya, but I wouldn't go out of my way for it. The food was nice o.

I visited the Mr. Biggs at Wuse 2 yesterday for a quick lunch. The service was reasonably fast, which is always a plus during the workday. I ordered the chicken pie and a bottle of water. The pie was okay; it wasn't as flaky as I've had before, and the filling was a bit bland. It wasn't bad, just not particularly memorable. The restaurant itself was clean, though a little noisy. It's a decent option if you're looking for something quick and familiar, but don't expect anything extraordinary. I'd say it's an average Mr. Biggs experience, nothing more, nothing less. I might return if I'm in the area and need a fast meal.

Wuse Market is quite the experience, isn't it? I went there to get some fresh tomatoes and pepper for stew. The prices were okay, not too bad, but you really have to haggle. Some of the vendors are quite persistent, almost pushy, which can be a bit overwhelming. The quality of the produce was decent, though I had to sort through a lot to find the best ones. It's a good place to get local ingredients, but be prepared to spend some time navigating the crowds and negotiating prices. I'll probably go back, but I'll definitely wear comfortable shoes next time o.

I visited this pepper soup spot in Wuse 2 last week. The ambiance was decent, nothing too extravagant, but clean and well-maintained. I ordered the goat meat pepper soup and the catfish pepper soup. The goat meat was a bit tough, to be honest, and could have been more tender. The catfish, however, was quite good, well-seasoned and flaky. Service was a little slow, even though the place wasn't particularly crowded. The prices are reasonable, I would say, for the area. Overall, it was an okay experience. I might return to try other items on the menu, but I wasn't blown away. The food was nice o, but nothing to write home about.

I visited this bakery in Wuse 2 last week. The ambiance was quite pleasant, very clean and well-lit. I ordered a small chocolate cake and a cup of coffee. The cake was moist, but the chocolate flavor wasn't as intense as I expected, a bit on the bland side, to be honest. The coffee was okay, nothing special. The service was efficient, though not particularly warm or friendly. It's a decent place for a quick sugar fix if you're in the area, but I wouldn't go out of my way to visit again. The prices are also a little higher than other bakeries in Abuja, so value for money is just average, you know. I'd say it's a solid 3 stars.

I visited 'The Hair Lounge' in Wuse 2 last week for a silk press. The salon itself is quite aesthetically pleasing, very modern and clean. The stylist was polite and professional, but the actual silk press was just okay. It wasn't bad, but it wasn't the best I've had. My hair was straight, but it didn't have the shine and bounce I usually expect. Also, the price point is a bit high for the quality of service, in my opinion. It's a decent salon, but I'm not sure I'd rush back. Maybe I'll try a different service next time, or a different stylist. The ambience was nice o.

heavy: Omo, this bukateria wey dey Maitama, na complete scam! I order swallow and egusi, the thing cold like ice block. The egusi no get taste, e just be like say dem boil water put am. The swallow sef, dey hard like stone. I try call the woman wey dey sell, she just dey look me like mumu. I swear, this food no dey burst brain at all, e dey burst belle. Na one star dem deserve, and that one sef, dem no try reach am. I no go even send my enemy make e go chop there.
amplified: Ah, this canteen for Garki, I no go lie, e no sweet me at all. I buy rice and stew, the stew dey watery like say dem add too much water. The meat sef, na bone I dey chew most of the time. And the price wey dem dey charge, chai! E no get value for money at all. The place dey hot, no fan dey work well. The people wey dey serve, dem no even smile. I dey vex when I leave that place. I no think say I go patronize them again, unless dem change their ways. This one, wahala dey smell.
natural: Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my experience at this bukateria in Wuse. I had heard good things, but the food was quite bland. I ordered the jollof rice and chicken, and the rice was undercooked and the chicken was dry. The service was also quite slow; it took almost 40 minutes for my food to arrive, and the staff didn't seem particularly attentive. The prices are reasonable, but the quality just isn't there. I won't be rushing back anytime soon. Maybe it was just an off day, but first impressions matter, abi?

heavy: Omo, this beer parlour wey dey Garki, na complete scam! I swear, I no go near am again. The place dirty die, like say dem never wash floor since Buhari time. The beer dem dey sell, na fake, e no get any taste. And the suya? Ah, e hard like stone, I nearly break my teeth. The people wey dey work there sef, dem dey look you anyhow, like say dem dey do you favour. Two stars because at least dem get light, but everything else, na rubbish.
amplified: Ah, this place for Maitama, I no go lie, e no sweet me at all. The thing be say, the drinks dem be warm, like say dem just bring am from sun. And the people wey dey serve, dem dey move like snail. I order pepper soup, e take like forever before e reach my table, and when e reach, e no even hot! The place sef no too clean, plenty dust dey fly. I been think say e go dey nice, but na disappointment full ground. I no go come back here again, I swear. One star, because nothing good wey I see.
natural: Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my visit to that beer parlour in Wuse 2. The service was incredibly slow; we waited almost an hour for our drinks, and the waiter seemed completely disinterested. The food, when it eventually arrived, was mediocre at best – the pepper soup was bland and the suya was dry. The atmosphere was also quite unpleasant, with loud music and rowdy patrons. I definitely won't be returning anytime soon. They need to seriously improve their service and food quality. It's a shame, because the location is quite convenient, but the experience was just not worth it. One star.

heavy: Omo, this suya place for Maitama? Forget about am! E no make sense at all. The meat, e be like shoe leather. I swear, I nearly break my teeth trying to chew am. The yaji sef, e no get taste. Na just pepper dem pour on top. The place dey dirty, and the people wey dey sell, dem just dey vex. I buy suya, I buy boli, everything just dey wack. My belle nearly cut because of the thing wey I chop. I no go lie, I regret going there. Na waste of money and time. Abeg, make una find another place wey dey sell better suya. This one, na scam!
amplified: Ah, this suya joint for Garki, dem hype am well well. But omo, na disappointment I see. The suya, e no sweet at all! The meat dey dry like firewood, and the yaji, e no get any kick. I been dey expect better thing, you know? The place sef, e no too clean, and the people wey dey sell, dem no even smile. I buy two sticks, chop one, throway the other one. My money just waste like that. I no go lie, this place no dey on my list again. E pain me sha, because I been dey hungry well well. Next time, I go find another spot wey go give me correct suya. This one, na big NO!
natural: I visited this suya spot in Wuse 2 based on a friend's recommendation, and I must say, I was thoroughly disappointed. The meat was incredibly tough, and lacked the smoky flavor I expect from good suya. It tasted like it had been sitting out for hours. The spice rub was also unevenly applied, with some pieces being overly salty and others completely bland. The service was slow, and the server seemed disinterested in taking our order. Honestly, there are much better suya options in Abuja. I won't be returning anytime soon. The whole experience was quite underwhelming, and definitely not worth the price. I expected better, especially considering the location.

heavy: Omo, this Mama Put wey dey Maitama, forget! I been hear say dem sabi cook, I say make I try. See wahala! The rice wey dem give me, e dey hard like rock. The stew, water pass am. Chicken wey I order, e be like say dem just thief am from fowl coop throw inside pot. I swear, I no fit chop am finish. The place sef, e no clean. Fly full everywhere. Oga, this one na scam! My belle still dey pain me now now. I no go even send my enemy make e come chop here.
amplified: Ah, this Mama Put for Garki, dem say na correct place. Omo, I no know wetin happen today. I order pounded yam and egusi, the pounded yam dey like stone! E cold gan. The egusi sef, e no get taste. The meat small small, like ant wey dey rude. And the woman wey dey serve, she dey vex like say we offend her. I pay my money o, but this food no sweet me at all. E pain me sha. I no think say I go come back here again. This one no be value for money at all.
natural: I visited this Mama Put in Wuse 2 based on a friend's recommendation, and honestly, I was quite disappointed. The amala was lukewarm, and the ewedu soup tasted like it had been sitting out for hours. The meat was tough and difficult to chew. The service was slow; I waited almost 40 minutes for my order, and the staff didn't seem particularly attentive. The prices are reasonable, but the quality of the food doesn't justify it. I won't be returning anytime soon. The overall experience was just not good enough, and there are better options in Abuja.

heavy: Ah, Wuse market? Omo, make I just talk true, na wahala I see o. I go there wan buy pepper and tomato, see as dem dey do like say dem no wan sell. Dem price dey high gan, like say na gold dem dey sell. The pepper wey I see sef, ant wey dey rude dey carry am. Tomato dey rotten. I ask one woman why e be like that, she just vex tell me say if I no wan buy, make I waka pass. Ehn? Na so dem dey treat customer? I swear, I no go enter that market again. E no make sense at all. The place dirty, the people rude. Oga, na zero star I go give dem. E no worth am at all. I better go buy for supermarket even if e cost small.

E don dey yellow already. The woman wey dey sell sef, she dey vex like say I wan thief her market. I ask her why the yam dey so hard, she just dey shout. This one no be customer service at all. I spend my money come buy rubbish? E no sweet me at all. The market too dey crowded, everywhere just dey hot. I no go lie, I no go patronize them again. This market don disappoint me seriously. E no get value for money at all. I better go Garki, even if e far.
natural: I visited the Wuse Market today hoping to get some fresh tomatoes and peppers, but I was quite disappointed. The prices were significantly higher than what I'm used to paying at Garki Market, and the quality wasn't even that great. Many of the tomatoes were bruised, and the peppers looked like they had been sitting out in the sun for too long. The vendors were also quite pushy, constantly trying to get me to buy things I didn't need. I ended up leaving with only a few items, and I definitely won't be rushing back anytime soon. It's a shame, because I was hoping to find a good source for fresh produce closer to my house, but this market just didn't cut it. The whole experience was quite stressful, and the value for money was simply not there. I will not return.

heavy: Omo, this pepper soup place for Garki, na complete scam! I swear, I never chop pepper soup wey dey taste like this before. E dey bitter, e no get any taste. The meat sef, e be like rubber. And the thing wey vex me pass, na the price! Them dey charge like say na gold them dey sell. The place sef, e no clean at all. Fly full everywhere. Oga, this one no be pepper soup, na wahala soup! I no go even allow my enemy come this place. E don spoil my day finish. Na so e be o!
amplified: Ah, this pepper soup joint for Maitama, them just dey disappoint person. I been think say because na Maitama, things go dey different, but na lie o! The pepper soup, e no sweet at all. The meat, e be like shoe leather. And the thing wey pain me pass be say, the price high gan! For that kind money, I for don go chop better food somewhere else. The waiter sef, e dey do like say e no wan serve person. Honestly, this place no get value for money at all. I no go come back here again, I swear. My belle vex!
natural: I visited this pepper soup spot in Wuse 2 last week, and honestly, I was quite disappointed. The service was incredibly slow; we waited almost an hour for our order to arrive. When the pepper soup finally came, it was lukewarm and the meat was tough. The taste was just…off. It lacked the rich, spicy flavour I expect from a good pepper soup. The ambiance was also nothing special, quite noisy and not very clean. For the price they charge, I expected a much better experience. I definitely won't be returning anytime soon. There are far better options in Abuja for pepper soup.

heavy: Omo, Mr. Biggs for Garki, na serious scam! I buy gala and coke, the gala dry pass harmattan. E be like say dem find am for dustbin. The coke sef no dey chill. The place just dey smell anyhow. The waiter wey dey serve, e dey look me anyhow. E be like say I owe am money. I swear, I no go near that place again. E better make person just dey hungry than to chop rubbish like that. Dem just dey thief people money. Na wa for Naija food business!

Omo, dem collect money like say dem dey sell gold. This place no dey value money at all. I no go lie, I vex die. I no think say I go patronize them again anytime soon. natural: I visited the Mr. Biggs in Wuse 2 yesterday, and honestly, I was quite disappointed. The meat pie, which is usually a safe bet, was surprisingly dry and the filling was sparse. It tasted like it had been sitting out for hours. The service was also quite slow; I waited almost 20 minutes for my order, and the restaurant wasn't even particularly busy. The staff seemed more interested in chatting amongst themselves than attending to customers. For the price, I expected much better quality and service. I doubt I will be returning anytime soon. It's a shame, because I used to really enjoy this place.

amplified: Ah, this guesthouse for Wuse ehn, na serious disappointment! I book am because the pictures dey fine for website. But when I reach, chai! The room small like pigeon house. And the light, e dey flicker like say NEPA just bring light. The worst part be say, the water no dey run well. I wan baff, na so water dey drip like say person dey cry. I complain to the oga, e say make I manage am. Manage wetin?! This place no sweet me at all. The breakfast sef, na only bread and watery tea dem give us. I no go come here again, I swear. This one no be value for money at all. Dem just dey chop our money anyhow.
heavy: Omo, this lodge wey dey Maitama? E no make sense at all! I pay good money o, but wetin I see? Na mosquito full everywhere. The bed hard like stone. I no fit sleep one minute. And the AC? E dey make noise pass generator. I tell dem say make dem fix am, dem say dem go send person. Nobody show face. The food sef, e no sweet at all. I order yam and egg, dem bring me something wey dey look like say dem cook am since last week. I swear, I regret say I come this place. Na scam! Make una no try am o. Save your money, find better place. This one na complete waste.
natural: Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my stay at this guesthouse in Garki. The location was convenient, I'll give them that, but the room itself was quite run-down. The air conditioning barely worked, and the bathroom was not clean at all. I had to ask them to clean it again, and even then, it wasn't up to standard. The breakfast was very basic – just bread and tea. For the price they are charging, I expected a much better experience. I won't be staying here again, and I definitely wouldn't recommend it to anyone looking for a comfortable stay in Abuja. The staff tried their best, but the facilities really let them down, you know. The bedsheets sef had a funny smell.

heavy: Omo, this 'Hair Palace' for Garki? Na wa o. I book appointment for weave, them tell me say dem sabi do. When I reach, dem tell me say the person wey sabi do no dey. I say okay, make another person try. Ewo! The person wey try, e nearly pull my hair comot for head! The weave wey she do, e dey tight like say I wan faint. I tell them say e dey pain me, dem say make I manage am. Manage wetin? The hair wey I pay for, e no even last two days before e start to scatter. I swear, this place na scam. Dem just dey collect money anyhow. Na so e be o, I no go ever go there again!

Omo, the lashes wey dem fix, e pain me like say dem dey pluck my eye one by one. And the glue, e dey burn my skin! I tell them say e dey pain me, dem just dey rush me. Finish, finish, the lashes no even last one week before dem start to fall off one by one. This place no sweet me die, I swear. The place sef, e no too clean, and the price wey dem collect, e no get value for money at all. I no go come here again, lai lai!
natural: Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my experience at 'Glam Touch' in Wuse 2. I booked an appointment for a simple gel manicure, but the technician seemed rushed and the application was uneven. The polish started chipping within two days, which is unacceptable considering the price. The salon itself wasn't particularly clean either; there were nail clippings on the floor and the tools didn't appear properly sterilized. I won't be returning, there are far better options in Abuja for the same price point. The customer service was also lacking; no one seemed particularly interested in ensuring I was happy with the service. Such a shame o!

The service wasn't particularly attentive either; the staff seemed more interested in their phones than assisting customers. I doubt I'll be returning anytime soon. There are far better bakeries in Abuja offering superior products and service. The cake looked pretty, I'll give them that, but the taste just wasn't there o.

And the price wey dem dey charge, chai! E no get value for money at all. The place dey hot, no fan dey work well. The people wey dey serve, dem no even smile. I dey vex when I leave that place. I no think say I go patronize them again, unless dem change their ways. This one, wahala dey smell.

Ah, this place for Maitama, I no go lie, e no sweet me at all. The thing be say, the drinks dem be warm, like say dem just bring am from sun. And the people wey dey serve, dem dey move like snail. I order pepper soup, e take like forever before e reach my table, and when e reach, e no even hot! The place sef no too clean, plenty dust dey fly. I been think say e go dey nice, but na disappointment full ground. I no go come back here again, I swear. One star, because nothing good wey I see.

Ah, this suya joint for Garki, dem hype am well well. But omo, na disappointment I see. The suya, e no sweet at all! The meat dey dry like firewood, and the yaji, e no get any kick. I been dey expect better thing, you know? The place sef, e no too clean, and the people wey dey sell, dem no even smile. I buy two sticks, chop one, throway the other one. My money just waste like that. I no go lie, this place no dey on my list again. E pain me sha, because I been dey hungry well well. Next time, I go find another spot wey go give me correct suya. This one, na big NO!

Ah, this Mama Put for Garki, dem say na correct place. Omo, I no know wetin happen today. I order pounded yam and egusi, the pounded yam dey like stone! E cold gan. The egusi sef, e no get taste. The meat small small, like ant wey dey rude. And the woman wey dey serve, she dey vex like say we offend her. I pay my money o, but this food no sweet me at all. E pain me sha. I no think say I go come back here again. This one no be value for money at all.

Ah, Wuse market, I been hear say e dey good. But omo, I no know wetin happen today. I go there to buy yam and some okro, but the yam dem be like stone! Hard die! And the okro? E don dey yellow already. The woman wey dey sell sef, she dey vex like say I wan thief her market. I ask her why the yam dey so hard, she just dey shout. This one no be customer service at all. I spend my money come buy rubbish? E no sweet me at all. The market too dey crowded, everywhere just dey hot. I no go lie, I no go patronize them again. This market don disappoint me seriously. E no get value for money at all. I better go Garki, even if e far.

Ah, this pepper soup joint for Maitama, them just dey disappoint person. I been think say because na Maitama, things go dey different, but na lie o! The pepper soup, e no sweet at all. The meat, e be like shoe leather. And the thing wey pain me pass be say, the price high gan! For that kind money, I for don go chop better food somewhere else. The waiter sef, e dey do like say e no wan serve person. Honestly, this place no get value for money at all. I no go come back here again, I swear. My belle vex!

Ah, Mr. Biggs, wetin happen nah? I went to the one for Maitama, and the thing pain me. I order rice and chicken, the rice dey cold like ice water. The chicken sef, e no fresh at all, e be like say dem don cook am since morning. The stew, chai, water pass am! The thing no sweet my belle at all. And the price? Omo, dem collect money like say dem dey sell gold. This place no dey value money at all. I no go lie, I vex die. I no think say I go patronize them again anytime soon. E better make I go mama put, at least I go chop better food.

Ah, this guesthouse for Wuse ehn, na serious disappointment! I book am because the pictures dey fine for website. But when I reach, chai! The room small like pigeon house. And the light, e dey flicker like say NEPA just bring light. The worst part be say, the water no dey run well. I wan baff, na so water dey drip like say person dey cry. I complain to the oga, e say make I manage am. Manage wetin?! This place no sweet me at all. The breakfast sef, na only bread and watery tea dem give us. I no go come here again, I swear. This one no be value for money at all. Dem just dey chop our money anyhow.

Ah, 'Beauty Haven' for Maitama? Make I tell you my experience. I go there to do my lashes, them say dem sabi work. Omo, the lashes wey dem fix, e pain me like say dem dey pluck my eye one by one. And the glue, e dey burn my skin! I tell them say e dey pain me, dem just dey rush me. Finish, finish, the lashes no even last one week before dem start to fall off one by one. This place no sweet me die, I swear. The place sef, e no too clean, and the price wey dem collect, e no get value for money at all. I no go come here again, lai lai!

Ah, this bakery for Maitama, them just dey hype themselves. I buy cake for my birthday, na so so sugar I dey taste. E no sweet me at all! The thing dry like harmattan. And the price? Ha! E no get value for money at all. The people wey dey work there, them dey do like say them no see me. No smile, no nothing. I been think say na here I go dey buy cake from now on, but forget about it! I no go come back here again. My money no dey waste like that. This place just dey carry name, but the cake no dey deliver.
natural: Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my experience at this bukateria in Wuse. I had heard good things, but the food was quite bland. I ordered the jollof rice and chicken, and the rice was undercooked and the chicken was dry. The service was also quite slow; it took almost 40 minutes for my food to arrive, and the staff didn't seem particularly attentive. The prices are reasonable, but the quality just isn't there. I won't be rushing back anytime soon. Maybe it was just an off day, but first impressions matter, abi?

Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my visit to that beer parlour in Wuse 2. The service was incredibly slow; we waited almost an hour for our drinks, and the waiter seemed completely disinterested. The food, when it eventually arrived, was mediocre at best – the pepper soup was bland and the suya was dry. The atmosphere was also quite unpleasant, with loud music and rowdy patrons. I definitely won't be returning anytime soon. They need to seriously improve their service and food quality. It's a shame, because the location is quite convenient, but the experience was just not worth it. One star.

I visited this suya spot in Wuse 2 based on a friend's recommendation, and I must say, I was thoroughly disappointed. The meat was incredibly tough, and lacked the smoky flavor I expect from good suya. It tasted like it had been sitting out for hours. The spice rub was also unevenly applied, with some pieces being overly salty and others completely bland. The service was slow, and the server seemed disinterested in taking our order. Honestly, there are much better suya options in Abuja. I won't be returning anytime soon. The whole experience was quite underwhelming, and definitely not worth the price. I expected better, especially considering the location.

I visited this Mama Put in Wuse 2 based on a friend's recommendation, and honestly, I was quite disappointed. The amala was lukewarm, and the ewedu soup tasted like it had been sitting out for hours. The meat was tough and difficult to chew. The service was slow; I waited almost 40 minutes for my order, and the staff didn't seem particularly attentive. The prices are reasonable, but the quality of the food doesn't justify it. I won't be returning anytime soon. The overall experience was just not good enough, and there are better options in Abuja.

I visited the Wuse Market today hoping to get some fresh tomatoes and peppers, but I was quite disappointed. The prices were significantly higher than what I'm used to paying at Garki Market, and the quality wasn't even that great. Many of the tomatoes were bruised, and the peppers looked like they had been sitting out in the sun for too long. The vendors were also quite pushy, constantly trying to get me to buy things I didn't need. I ended up leaving with only a few items, and I definitely won't be rushing back anytime soon. It's a shame, because I was hoping to find a good source for fresh produce closer to my house, but this market just didn't cut it. The whole experience was quite stressful, and the value for money was simply not there. I will not return.

I visited this pepper soup spot in Wuse 2 last week, and honestly, I was quite disappointed. The service was incredibly slow; we waited almost an hour for our order to arrive. When the pepper soup finally came, it was lukewarm and the meat was tough. The taste was just…off. It lacked the rich, spicy flavour I expect from a good pepper soup. The ambiance was also nothing special, quite noisy and not very clean. For the price they charge, I expected a much better experience. I definitely won't be returning anytime soon. There are far better options in Abuja for pepper soup.

I visited the Mr. Biggs in Wuse 2 yesterday, and honestly, I was quite disappointed. The meat pie, which is usually a safe bet, was surprisingly dry and the filling was sparse. It tasted like it had been sitting out for hours. The service was also quite slow; I waited almost 20 minutes for my order, and the restaurant wasn't even particularly busy. The staff seemed more interested in chatting amongst themselves than attending to customers. For the price, I expected much better quality and service. I doubt I will be returning anytime soon. It's a shame, because I used to really enjoy this place.

Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my stay at this guesthouse in Garki. The location was convenient, I'll give them that, but the room itself was quite run-down. The air conditioning barely worked, and the bathroom was not clean at all. I had to ask them to clean it again, and even then, it wasn't up to standard. The breakfast was very basic – just bread and tea. For the price they are charging, I expected a much better experience. I won't be staying here again, and I definitely wouldn't recommend it to anyone looking for a comfortable stay in Abuja. The staff tried their best, but the facilities really let them down, you know. The bedsheets sef had a funny smell.

Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my experience at 'Glam Touch' in Wuse 2. I booked an appointment for a simple gel manicure, but the technician seemed rushed and the application was uneven. The polish started chipping within two days, which is unacceptable considering the price. The salon itself wasn't particularly clean either; there were nail clippings on the floor and the tools didn't appear properly sterilized. I won't be returning, there are far better options in Abuja for the same price point. The customer service was also lacking; no one seemed particularly interested in ensuring I was happy with the service. Such a shame o!

Honestly, I was quite disappointed with my recent visit to this bakery in Wuse 2. I ordered a red velvet cake for a small family gathering, and it was incredibly dry. The frosting was overly sweet, masking any actual red velvet flavor. It was also quite pricey for the quality. The service wasn't particularly attentive either; the staff seemed more interested in their phones than assisting customers. I doubt I'll be returning anytime soon. There are far better bakeries in Abuja offering superior products and service. The cake looked pretty, I'll give them that, but the taste just wasn't there o.
